name: Expeditioner's Jǫklumarkar
line:
All the provisions and preparations are in place, and the team has gathered.
You and the explorers depart the southernmost city of Sami for the north.
Make your way through Sami and past the icefield, and the veil that shrouds the unknown will be lifted.

zones.name: Brisk Marshlands
zones.desc: From here you step into nature. Pure white snow falls onto the water, a chill breeze pushes your boat, and roaming around always makes one lose track of time.

zones.name: Placid Forests
zones.desc: The Hornbeasts in the thick forest lie down, inviting the visitors to rest together. The pitch-black shadows of the trees silently stain the snow, but the prey is completely unaware.

zones.name: Dawning Frostbounds
zones.desc: The bitter cold pierces through your marrow, with the forest gradually thinning as the black tundra separates the sparse specks of light. The roadmap is already blank, and whose guidance can we trust?

zones.name: Gelid Rockshelves
zones.desc: The expeditioners' line of sight has been cut off by the darkness of the north. Resilient Sami lifts Its mountain range as the cold, stone-hearted warriors construct towering walls. Do you still wish to continue onward?

zones.name: Immaculate Garden
zones.desc: Across the mountains, life is silent. Since antiquity, the solid ice has sealed up countless lives, propagating flower-like crystals.

zones.name: Clairvoyant Carcass
zones.time: An inescapable fate has stood at the end of the icefield for a long time,
zones.desc: and now, everyone can see it.

zones.name: Dust of Eternity
zones.desc: Finite lives measure infinite time, the expeditioners' footsteps lifting sand and dust from antiquity.

nodeTypes.name: Combat Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Everyone has their weapons drawn. They're definitely not here to negotiate.
nodeTypes.func: A standard combat node. Win to earn collectibles, Originium Ingots, and other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Some violence may well exceed your expectations, a very common occurrence.
nodeTypes.func: A harder fight with stronger enemies than regular Combat Ops — and better rewards (Hope, etc.) to match.

nodeTypes.name: Dreadful Foe
nodeTypes.desc: Criminals, ruffians, and the damned… You're in trouble.
nodeTypes.func: The boss battle guarding the end of each floor. You must clear it to move on to the next floor.

nodeTypes.name: Safe House
nodeTypes.desc: Nobody will bother you here, and there's plenty of food and water. No need to be so tense all the time.
nodeTypes.func: A rest node offering upkeep options such as restoring Objective HP or temporarily promoting Operators.

nodeTypes.name: Encounter
nodeTypes.desc: New friends, old rivals… Strange encounters never age. Only scars stand the test of time.
nodeTypes.func: A random-event node. Depending on your choices you gain rewards — or pay a price.

nodeTypes.name: Downtime Recreation
nodeTypes.desc: A little indulgence in life's simpler pleasures goes a long way in fighting the monotony and hardships on your long road ahead.
nodeTypes.func: A recreation node with theme-specific minigames and wagers. Rewards depend on the outcome.

nodeTypes.name: Dense Fog
nodeTypes.desc: There's something in the mist, but you cannot see anything...
nodeTypes.func: An unidentified node hidden from view. Its true type is revealed when you enter.

nodeTypes.name: Wish Fulfilled
nodeTypes.desc: There are many ways of making a wish, but the wish itself is almost always the same thing.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you pick one recruitment voucher from a random selection.

nodeTypes.name: Lost and Found
nodeTypes.desc: The void of helplessness will be filled by the joy and satisfaction of gain.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you trade what you have (Objective HP, collectibles, etc.) for other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Scout
nodeTypes.desc: Someone will always have to take the first step, to show the way for those who come after.
nodeTypes.func: A node that sends some of your Operators on a detached mission. They leave your squad for a while and bring back rewards on return.

nodeTypes.name: Rogue Trader
nodeTypes.desc: Neither an ally nor an enemy. Some people like to sit on the fence.
nodeTypes.func: A shop where you spend Originium Ingots on collectibles and items, or restore Objective HP.

nodeTypes.name: Bosky Passage
nodeTypes.desc: Branches on one side and leaves on the other, the unexplored land lies right next to you.
nodeTypes.func: An entrance to the hidden realm. Realm-exclusive encounters can appear there at random.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc:
Are your feet on the road, the mud, or in the snow? 
This node will always be in a regular state and cannot be targeted by Foldartals
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc:
Are your feet on the road, the mud, or in the snow? 
This node will always be in a regular state and cannot be targeted by Foldartals
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Start with higher Max Life Points, and Collapsal Paradigms intensify at a slower pace

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Collapsal Paradigms are more likely to intensify when Collapse value is at 12 or higher

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Clearing a stage without earning Perfect will increase Collapse value by 1

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +5% ATK and +5% Max HP

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: All 4-star and above Operators cost +1 Hope to recruit

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: All enemies take 5% less Physical and Arts damage

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Leader enemies take 5% less Physical and Arts damage

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Anti-Interference Index -1 upon entering the 1st, 3rd and 5th Floors

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: Elite and Leader enemies take 5% less Physical and Arts damage

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +5% DEF and +5% Max HP

difficulties.name: Braving Nature
difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +10% ATK, DEF, and Max HP. Upon entering a new floor, the increment in Collapse Value is further increased by 1

difficulties.name: Expeditioner Record
difficulties.rule: Monthly squads are refreshed on the first day of each month

difficulties.name: Deep Investigation
difficulties.rule: A challenge mode that provides a unique experience.

stages.name: Prisoners' Night
stages.desc:
The worthless, shackled Columbian death-row inmates sink into Sami's swamp. Afterwards, the strange sound of chains being dragged on the ground can be often heard in the wetlands.
 that can block the enemy's route are present on the field

stages.name: Ghosts of the Resort
stages.desc: The elder brother drowned in the bathtub, the younger brother choked to death on an apple, the mercenary guarding the door was impaled by thorns, and this house was abandoned.

stages.name: Hands of Moss
stages.desc: Handprints made of moss appear on walls, tree trunks, and clothing. The Siracusan Mafia's favorite way of leaving their mark has shown up in Sami, in a unique form.

stages.name: Beasts Awaiting Slaughter
stages.desc: At some point, the leader of the beast pack was replaced by a monster of a human. The beasts tremble with fear, but still they obey the boss's orders.

stages.name: No More Than Four
stages.desc: Following three, preceding five—a certain number has become taboo here. The mere mention enrages those present, and they will vent their anger with violence.

stages.name: Path with No End
stages.desc: Outsiders find it hard to distinguish Sami's environments, so they walk circles through what they believe are the same areas over and over, never finding an exit.

stages.name: Low-Altitude Maneuvering
stages.desc:
This patch of forest has been transformed into a low-altitude flight test site, and the person in charge warmly invites you and your research team to participate in their maneuvering exercise.
 that generate airflow currents are present on the field

stages.name: Shadows With Ghosts
stages.desc: The projector left behind in the forest occasionally reflects a few familiar looking phantoms, but no one notices, and one by one the shadows walk out of the device and into the forest.

stages.name: Unwell
stages.desc:
It's said that there will always be someone on the team who the others don't care for, and they will be quietly replaced as the squad walks a few extra rounds through the mountain forest, their teammates completely unaware.
 that grant Max DP are present on the field

stages.name: Don't Turn Around, Sluggies
stages.desc: The Originium slugs crawl forward, completely unaware that something is following them from behind.

stages.name: Payback
stages.desc:
Many years ago, foreigners devastated the woods with heavy machinery. Many years later, foreigners flee the woods frightened by phantom rumbling. 
There are  on the map that will deplete Life Points when destroyed

stages.name: Pretense Turned True
stages.desc: Don't spread lies in Sami. When someone takes one as truth, it becomes reality.

stages.name: Altar of Craving
stages.desc:
It is precisely because of the fuel it extracts from every passerby that this Sarkaz altar has thus far been able to operate unattended.
 that deal damage to both allies and enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Crafty Beasts' Nine-Caves
stages.desc: There is a kind of wild beast that, according to rumors, prepares nine exits in its lair for escape purposes. But the problem right now is that you're stuck in its den and don't know where the enemy will emerge from.

stages.name: Shady Frozen Sea
stages.desc:
You're certainly aware that the ocean and sailors won't show up on the icefield, and yet you don't think anything is off. Cognitive errors are the most common Collapsal Paradigm to manifest.
 tiles that reduce the DEF of allied units by half are present on the field

stages.name: So Close and Yet So Far
stages.desc: The relative distances between the mountains have been folded and stretched. What originally would take only a single step to reach now takes a long time.

stages.name: Rational Fracture
stages.desc: The enemy in front of you is moving so fast they resemble a high-latency image. You fret over how to deal with the matter, but just as you're about to scratch your head, you realize the hand you just raised is already on your back.

stages.name: Harass With Strength
stages.desc: Five lumberjacks got together and cursed their fellow workers to turn into slugs. The good news is that the workers did indeed become Originium Slugs. The bad news is that they will never get to see humanity ever again.

stages.name: Company Entanglement
stages.desc: Even though Columbia's tech giants have signed a contract for a joint-expedition of the icefield, their long-standing grievances will inevitably have some wanting to employ unfair means to attack their rivals.

stages.name: Practical Test
stages.desc:
This Rhine research station is currently stress-testing their technology in the cold environment. Your team has the privilege to be among the first batch of practical testers.
 that activate by absorbing experimental products are present on the field

stages.name: Collapsals' Afternoon
stages.desc:
You feel as if your team resembles a giant Originium slug that has been dropped onto a beach in Dossoles, and those Collapsals were originally tourists here on vacation.
 tiles that reduce allied units's DEF by half are present on the field

stages.name: The Big Migration
stages.desc: The animals just want to avoid the disasters in the north, and you're the only thing standing in their way to the south.

stages.name: Defense Substituting Offense
stages.desc:
The husks have you trapped within a heavily battered fortress, but perhaps the resources scattered around here might let you survive this next wave of attacks.
 that can block the enemy's route are present on the field

stages.name: Noise Disturbance
stages.desc:
Ever since you got communication access on the icefield, you've always been able to hear a handful of nonsense words mixed in with the other person talking, but you didn't take it seriously—until the other expedition team took you for a pack of beasts.
 that deploy enemy mercenary drones are present on the field

stages.name: Restricted Zone
stages.desc:
Ursus will not listen to your excuses: as long as you enter their established restricted area, they will use force to expel you.
 that prevent allied units from being targeted by ranged enemy attacks are present on the field

stages.name: Where Ice Condenses
stages.desc: As long as there is even a trace of warmth flowing in your veins, you should not come here. For this place is only for the cold-blooded.

stages.name: Musical Disaster
stages.desc:
The tragically distorted tunes and sounds have become the group that in your mind best understands the music. They will use this string removed from the score to slice you up alive.
Allied units have their natural SP recovery speed reduced by , but can be restored to normal with

stages.name: Stay Away From Strangers
stages.desc:
Trees rotting in the ground, steel and dead flies in the sky. Life is not welcome here.
 that produce Rot Eaters are present on the field

stages.name: Confusing Appearance
stages.desc: Don't be deceived by their irregular appearance. Hold onto your thoughts and the battlefront, and don't give them an opening.

stages.name: Requested Enemies Obtained
stages.desc: When you see a group of indistinct shadows on the icefield from a distance, never imagine the worst case scenario. After all, you don't want your 'Wish Fulfilled' in such an environment.

stages.name: March of the Dead
stages.desc: Your most life-enriching understanding of death comes from the never resting Dublinn army.

stages.name: Where be No Mounts and Seas
stages.desc:
The land is vast, the sea is endless, and Feranmuts naturally exist in places other than Yan. So these people cross the snowcapped mountains and sail the rivers, all to look for the ancient beings hiding in the cracks.
 that strengthen enemies are present on the field

stages.name: Instinct Contamination
stages.desc:
The husks treat all living things equally.
 tiles that cause units on them to gradually lose HP but greatly increases their ATK and ASPD are present on the field

stages.name: Manmade Carnival
stages.desc: The living stay away! Non-persons also stay away! The machines have become their own masters, and are hunting down every carbon-based organism without a microchip!

stages.name: Frost and Sand
stages.desc: Two races living at the entrance to the land's end have appeared here in the form of husks. Perhaps the ends of both the icefield and the desert are not dissimilar.

stages.name: Terminus of Life
stages.desc:
The existence of 'Frozen Monstrosities' is itself a warning: If you don't fight for your life, you will end up just like it.
 that generate airflow currents are present on the field

stages.desc:

stages.name: Blade Signifier
stages.desc:
It spreads a black carpet of death, and those unable to kill it shall walk alongside it.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Breathing
stages.desc:
Breathe in fear, exhale despair, and so the Dominion spreads on towards Ursus.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: New Tribe
stages.desc:
A clan without a tree or a name takes form. A former leader of many still remains as chief. They are the demons' vanguard forces.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Tree-Snatcher
stages.desc:
It draws in the husks, preparing to rot every last Sami clan tree. And afterwards, Sami will sink into nothingness.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Nature's Wrath
stages.desc:
Lava bursts forth from Sami's surface, and the plants awake from their slumber to attack all foreign enemies. All signs point to Sami's unrestrained anger.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: The Earth Wakes
stages.desc:
The most ancient of plants have also uprooted themselves and are heading north. Perhaps, when the time is ripe, the land itself will rise up to destroy the threat from the north.
 that can block the enemy's route are present on the field
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Lofty Silverfrost
stages.desc:
The earth rises, testing the will of those who have come. Only by gaining the earth's approval will we be able to head further north.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Ice-Cold Image
stages.desc:
Cold and ice are merely manifestations of nature. How can the land invite you to face the unconventional enemy in the north if you can't even overcome this? 
[Surge of Nature] becomes a  that inflicts Cold throughout the entire map,  gain increased HP 
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Sami's Entropy
stages.desc:
The hero of Sami has failed, and become the peoples' greatest threat. If the demons are not eliminated quickly, such a cycle will only continue until Sami is destroyed.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Hollow Visage
stages.desc:
No matter what long-cherished aspirations this Sami hero once had, he is now but a collapsed symbol stripped of his self—just like the thousands of Ursus behind him. 
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Gardener
stages.desc:
It burrows through the cracks in the circular plate, sowing seeds of 'nothing' into things of 'being.' Driving it away will allow you to walk through the garden safe and sound, to the land's end.
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Sentinel
stages.desc:
It opens its vacuous eye, examining everything in this dimension. Only when the eye shuts will humanity get to chance a glimpse at the secret beyond the ends of the earth. 
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Sands of Time
stages.desc:
At the end of all existence, time and space have lost meaning, leaving two kings, two armies, and an endless struggle. 
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Enter Eternity
stages.desc:
The footnotes of time have been removed, and the strife bears no fruit. Their war will continue on, but where will you go? 
This node will always be in a regular state and node type cannot be changed

stages.name: Halfway on Khaganquest
stages.desc: These shadows all come from the same persistent Nightzmora. He has long since gone astray, but the shadows left by his obsession still relentlessly march northward on a punitive expedition.

stages.name: Long-sought Compensation
stages.desc: The audience are not demons, so they should be able to reach that beautiful happy end in their heads. Well, at least they lived and died together, so what more can they ask for?

stages.name: Penalty
stages.desc: The master of this house is clearly an expert at teaching disobedient guests a lesson.

stages.name: Nameless Hero
stages.desc: After being corrupted, some powerful Sami will turn into terrifying symbols of inhumanity, wantonly scattering the seeds of nothingness. But the most threatening are the heroes who have been stripped of all their qualities and reduced to mediocrity.

stages.name: Rock and a Hard Place
stages.desc: You are sure that this place has been influenced by the Collapsals. But the question is, how do you deal with invisible and intangible enemies hiding within the hearts of others?

stages.name: Long-term Trial
stages.desc: You file an application with Cannot to 'try out' these items until the end of the current expedition. He doesn't seem so willing to agree? No matter, you will always have ways to make him nod.

stages.name: Nature's Clause
stages.desc: You signed an agreement with Cannot without his knowledge, the terms allowing you to take all his goods. After all, nature is all about survival of the fittest, and since we're in Sami, Cannot will agree.

stages.name: Illusions In The Sand
stages.desc:
The Banshee doesn't wait for your response before placing you in the heart of a desert devastated by the winds. In the distance, a golden garden seems to appear with scarlet army tents. 
A  will be blowing in a random direction, but  can reinforce  to make them immune to the effects of sandstorms. This stage also has a  that can disable the Invisibility effect of nearby enemies

stages.name: Ancient Enemies
stages.desc: Ancient nightmares appear from the gate, and there are three of them.

stages.name: Half-baked Trip
stages.desc: Encouraged by his detective uncle, he summons his courage and follows his boss to Sami. But after he accidentally gets separated from the others, he immediately returns to his original submissive persona.

stages.name: Moneymaking from Nothing
stages.desc:
The Duck Lord manipulated these Collapsals to congregate here, roving around in a circle. A patch of land that should not be in Sami does appear dead-center in the altar, but has yet to bring any riches.
 that periodically deal damage to units above them are present on the field

stages.name: Luxurious Fleet
stages.desc: A slew of personal bodyguards to wealthy men emerge from the cars, but you know very well the biggest threat is not them.

stages.name: 'Envoy of Justice'
stages.desc: And thus, you let out a huge yell, and jump into a Columbian production crew currently filming on location in Sami.

enemy_2001_duckmi.name: Duck Lord
enemy_2001_duckmi.desc: A famous Columbian investor, entrepreneur, and master of 'Duck Power.' Out of the public eye, enjoys kicking back with others...
enemy_2001_duckmi.ability:
Cannot be blocked.
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after being attacked

enemy_2002_bearmi.name: Gopnik
enemy_2002_bearmi.desc: The Ursus security guard hired by Duck Lord. Powerful, but not very smart. Mainly responsible for carrying luggage for Duck Lord.

enemy_2034_sythef.name: Crying Thief
enemy_2034_sythef.desc: A Perro who left his family to seek out a new life... but all that people know is that he is always weeping.

enemy_1116_liprr.name: Ordinary Prisoner
enemy_1116_liprr.desc: A commonly seen prisoner of Mansfield State Prison. Even so, don't let your guard down or he'll slip past you.
enemy_1116_liprr.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK

enemy_1116_liprr_2.name: Seasoned Prisoner
enemy_1116_liprr_2.desc: An inmate deeply familiar with the laws of survival of Mansfield State Prison. Will utilize his accumulated experience to outsmart you and is difficult to deal with.
enemy_1116_liprr_2.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK

enemy_1118_lidbox.name: Pugilist Prisoner
enemy_1118_lidbox.desc: An inmate who puts his trust in his fists. ——In reality, the metal chains on his wrists are doing most of the work.
enemy_1118_lidbox.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and attacks ignore a certain portion of the target's DEF

enemy_2059_smbox.name: Testaceous Vinecreeps
enemy_2059_smbox.desc: A seed falls into a box abandoned in the wild, and soon becomes a hunter preying on curiosity.

enemy_2062_smcar.name: Self-Driving Recon Cart
enemy_2062_smcar.desc: When colliding with an enemy, inflicts Physical damage and gradually increases Recon proficiency.
enemy_2062_smcar.ability: When colliding with an enemy, inflicts Physical damage and gradually increases Recon proficiency.

enemy_1007_slime.name: Originium Slug
enemy_1007_slime.desc: An infected wild creature. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1007_slime_2.name: Originium Slug α
enemy_1007_slime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than a normal Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1075_dmgswd.name: Sarkaz Bladeweaver
enemy_1075_dmgswd.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. The Originium flowing through their blood allows them to hold these crudely modified Arts-imbued greatswords. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they are capable of dealing melee attacks that inflict Arts damage while in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1075_dmgswd.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and inflicts Arts damage with melee attacks.

enemy_1101_plkght.name: Roar Knightclub Trainee
enemy_1101_plkght.desc: Wearing the high-performance, lightweight armor provided by Roar Guards, this new batch of experimental armor has some humorous deficiencies that made these people into pitiful test subjects. 'Roar Guards, even the winds will bow to you!'
enemy_1101_plkght.ability: Has significantly increased DEF and RES for a period of time

enemy_1074_dbskar.name: Sarkaz Grudgebearer
enemy_1074_dbskar.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. Their Originium-wracked bodies have become convenient tools in their operations, allowing them to unleash Arts without relying on a staff. Though they won't attack while on Standby, they will cruelly expend their own life to complete their mission once in Combat-Ready mode.
enemy_1074_dbskar.ability: Starts battle in Standby and will not attack; upon being injured, becomes Combat-Ready and attacks two targets with long-ranged Arts damage.

enemy_1277_sgman.name: Famiglia Soldier
enemy_1277_sgman.desc: Low-level mafiosi who obey the orders of their superiors unquestioningly. Their lives are hardly worth anything, but their deaths are sufficient cause for their famiglia to seek revenge.
enemy_1277_sgman.ability: Increases Blood Debt by a miniscule amount when defeated

enemy_1278_sgbow.name: Famiglia Crossbowman
enemy_1278_sgbow.desc: Famiglia-trained crossbowmen, capable of striking from a distance. Crossbows for famiglia use are extremely sophisticated; when given emergency orders, these snipers will use basic Arts to complete their missions, not knowing where that knowledge came from.
enemy_1278_sgbow.ability: While under the Asset Liquidation status, attacks are converted into Arts damage

enemy_1277_sgman_2.name: Elite Famiglia Soldier
enemy_1277_sgman_2.desc: Battle-hardened, low-level mafiosi who are strong and swift-footed, worthy of being called elite fighters. When they are buried, the famiglia might send a letter of condolence home, nothing more.
enemy_1277_sgman_2.ability: Increases Blood Debt by a miniscule amount when defeated

enemy_1034_laxe.name: Butcher
enemy_1034_laxe.desc: An enemy of unknown identity who wears white industrial overalls and attacks with a massive blunt object. Their appearances and combat style readily reminds people of those serial killers from movies, hence their nickname - Butcher.

enemy_1034_laxe_2.name: Veteran Butcher
enemy_1034_laxe_2.desc: An enemy of unknown identity who wears white industrial overalls and attacks with a massive blunt object. Their hoods have been dyed red to symbolize greater strength and power compared to the rest of their ranks. It is only fitting that their nickname also reflects this.

enemy_2043_smsbr.name: Tainted Carcass
enemy_2043_smsbr.desc: A Sami warrior irreversibly corrupted by demons. He once went to the snow fields with a clear purpose, but is now completely bereft.
enemy_2043_smsbr.ability: Movement Speed significantly increases for a short time after taking damage

enemy_1307_mhrhcr.name: Basorobeast
enemy_1307_mhrhcr.desc: An herbivore that dwells by cliffsides. The protective boulders that crown their heads are, in fact, the secretions of a symbiotic organism. Some have witnessed Basorobeasts battling Chitotex Clawbeasts in turf wars.
enemy_1307_mhrhcr.ability: Weak spot located on its rear, damage dealt to the front is significantly decreased

enemy_1303_mhshep.name: Hornbeast
enemy_1303_mhshep.desc: An herbivore that moves in herds, common to the mountains. Docile creatures, they hide from predators using the mock-vegetation wool on their backs, but that's no reason to underestimate their pointed horns.
enemy_1303_mhshep.ability: Starts battle Resting. After being attacked, it and all surrounding hornbeasts become Combat-Ready

enemy_1000_gopro_2.name: Hound Pro
enemy_1000_gopro_2.desc: A creature controlled by a Reunion recon squad that is more dangerous than a normal Hound. Monitoring equipment is strapped to its body. Due to their speed and evasiveness, they can easily slip through enemy defenses and cause heavy damage.

enemy_1169_duphlx.name: Dublinn Phalanx Infantry
enemy_1169_duphlx.desc: A phalanx infantry in the Dublinn forces. The phalanx tactics they utilize evolved from the combat formations used in Gaul. In the phalanx, nearby infantry will support one another, increasing the might of the formation.
enemy_1169_duphlx.ability:
When other Phalanx Infantry are nearby, DEF increases (stacks)
Refraction

enemy_1169_duphlx_2.name: Dublinn Phalanx Commander
enemy_1169_duphlx_2.desc: A phalanx commander in the Dublinn forces, they organize the infantry under their command into phalanx formations. The phalanx tactics they utilize evolved from the combat formations used in Gaul. In the phalanx, nearby infantry will support one another, increasing the might of the formation.
enemy_1169_duphlx_2.ability:
When other Phalanx Commanders are nearby, DEF increases (stacks)
Refraction

enemy_1168_dumage.name: Dublinn Shadowcaster
enemy_1168_dumage.desc: A caster in the Dublinn forces. Their Originium Arts do not stand out, but their innovative breakthrough in mass-producing Arts-resistant masks has placed them in good standing amongst the Dublinn forces.

enemy_1036_amraoe.name: Shielded Senior Caster
enemy_1036_amraoe.desc: One of Reunion's elite Casters. They use complex Area-of-Effect Arts, making them more potent than normal Casters. They also seem to have fortified their defenses with Arts. and must be handled with caution. Due to their special state, they may be more vulnerable to Arts damage.
enemy_1036_amraoe.ability: Deals area damage to Operators on the four cardinal tiles next to the primary target.

enemy_1007_slime_3.name: Originium Slug β
enemy_1007_slime_3.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than an Originium Slug α. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1111_ucommd.name: Ursus Raider
enemy_1111_ucommd.desc: Special forces within the Ursus Army that strike a balance between mobility and breakthrough capabilities. Good at fighting underequipped enemy forces.
enemy_1111_ucommd.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 2 Block
Can use long-ranged weapons to attack  and

enemy_1049_eagent.name: Agent
enemy_1049_eagent.desc: A caster who utilizes coordinated tactics. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with Arts damage, and employ a variety of techniques to break through unprepared defenders.
enemy_1049_eagent.ability: Attacks temporarily reduce allied units' Block

enemy_1047_sagent.name: Sniper
enemy_1047_sagent.desc: A basic ranged soldier. They have very long range. Their sniper rifles can launch special ammunition capable of stunning our Operators.
enemy_1047_sagent.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1039_breakr.name: Breaker
enemy_1039_breakr.desc: A well-trained enemy unit that spearheads assaults. Though their attack power is only slightly higher than average, their terrifying speed often allows them to break through the defenses of the unprepared- hence their nickname, 'Breakers.'

enemy_1325_cbgpro.name: Military Hound
enemy_1325_cbgpro.desc: Military hounds that first saw action in Bolívar. Their claws and fangs are as vicious as their temperament.

enemy_1327_cbrokt.name: Arc Commando
enemy_1327_cbrokt.desc: Soldiers from the experimental unit born of the 'Horizon Arc Project,' able to fly at low altitudes with their jet propulsion equipment.
enemy_1327_cbrokt.ability: If Movement Speed is higher than starting speed, gains Low-Altitude Hovering and takes off!

enemy_1327_cbrokt_2.name: Arc Commando Captain
enemy_1327_cbrokt_2.desc: Soldiers from the experimental unit born of the 'Horizon Arc Project,' skilled at flying with their jet propulsion equipment. They enjoy the feeling of defying gravity.
enemy_1327_cbrokt_2.ability: If Movement Speed is higher than starting speed, gains Low-Altitude Hovering and takes off!

enemy_1329_cbshld.name: Arc Screenguard
enemy_1329_cbshld.desc: Soldiers from an experimental unit with formidable defenses using the most advanced alloy technologies. However, the unresolved instability of the material makes them better suited for short, high-pressure skirmishes than drawn-out battles.
enemy_1329_cbshld.ability: Starting DEF and RES are high, but reduced whenever damage is taken

enemy_1002_nsabr.name: Soldier
enemy_1002_nsabr.desc: One of Reunion's basic melee soldiers. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they wear masks to conceal their identities during operations.

enemy_1136_redace.name: 'Redmark' Infiltrator
enemy_1136_redace.desc: A special mercenary infiltrator who carries high-tech projection equipment, specializing in infiltrating enemy forces within harsh environments. 'Redmark Contracts' are tailor-made for elite and powerful mercenaries with a distinct lack of morals. The initiators of such contracts are ruthless and definitely up to no good.
enemy_1136_redace.ability:
Creates a  upon taking damage for the first time
While the  exists, cannot be blocked and becomes less likely to be attacked
Gains Physical and Arts Dodge while within the

enemy_1139_hologc.name: Holographic Mirage
enemy_1139_hologc.desc: Tactical Originium Equipment developed by a certain Columbian tech company. Generates a realistic holographic projection to obstruct enemies.

enemy_1314_wdnjd.name: Pathfinder Spy
enemy_1314_wdnjd.desc: A spy hired by a mysterious employer to monitor their rivals while disguised as civilians. Most are amateurs at gathering intelligence, and their impulsiveness and unorthodox practices often expose the secrets they think they're keeping safe.
enemy_1314_wdnjd.ability:
Spy
When their identity is revealed, the next attack's ATK is greatly increased

enemy_1313_wdfmr.name: Norport Civilian
enemy_1313_wdfmr.desc: Ordinary folk scattered throughout Norport Borough. Abandoned by Londinium and subjected to the Sarkaz's high-handed blockade, these orphans have become the ones most deeply affected by the haze of war.
enemy_1313_wdfmr.ability:
Friendly Unit
Once their identity is confirmed, restores your max DP after retreating to the Objective Point

enemy_1315_wdyjd.name: Sentry Spy
enemy_1315_wdyjd.desc: A spy hired by a mysterious employer to monitor their rivals while disguised as civilians. They have obviously undergone professional intelligence training, and the complexity of the blockade taught them even more about carefully observing their environment.
enemy_1315_wdyjd.ability:
Spy
When their identity is revealed, becomes Invisible

enemy_1319_wdfmpm.name: Rebellion Instigator
enemy_1319_wdfmpm.desc: Disruptors that constantly bypass the blockade. They weaken the enemy's forces from within by eagerly sowing fear among the masses, who in their panic break down the borough defenses all on their own.
enemy_1319_wdfmpm.ability: When not blocked, all unrevealed Civilians or Spies cannot be blocked

enemy_1320_wdrrl.name: Sarkaz Absurdist Tyrant
enemy_1320_wdrrl.desc: A product of the cooperation between the Damazti Cluster and the Military Commission. Under the guidance of the Damazti Cluster, these Sarkaz soldiers have learned to use transformation Arts to appear more intimidating. However, the body affects the mind, and their lack of rationality makes them even more brutal in battle.
enemy_1320_wdrrl.ability:
Can only be blocked by Operators with 3 or more Block
First attack deals Splash damage

enemy_2044_smwiz.name: Distorted Caster
enemy_2044_smwiz.desc: A Sami shaman possessed by demons. Everything they understood about nature has been reconstructed into pure nothingness.
enemy_2044_smwiz.ability: Movement Speed significantly increases after not attacking for a period of time, resets on attack

enemy_1021_bslime.name: Infused Originium Slug
enemy_1021_bslime.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1021_bslime_2.name: Infused Originium Slug α
enemy_1021_bslime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. More dangerous than a normal Infused Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime_2.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1026_aghost.name: Wraith Leader
enemy_1026_aghost.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. Due to being better trained and more heavily equipped, they are harder to neutralize in time, even if they have been detected. They utilize their agility and cunning to slip through defenses in order to sabotage mechanical and electrical equipment.

enemy_1267_nhpbr.name: Sarkaz Witherer
enemy_1267_nhpbr.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. Death cannot stop their corrupting presence, as their corpses continue to corrupt the land.
enemy_1267_nhpbr.ability: Releases Corruption when defeated, causing your nearby units to continuously lose HP

enemy_1273_stmgun.name: Londinium Mobile Defense Artillery
enemy_1273_stmgun.desc: Part of Londinium's city defenses. Has a large attack area. Built by the city's workers in their factories.
enemy_1273_stmgun.ability:
Capable of long-range attacks
Locks on to the target with the highest max HP and bombards it several times, dealing Arts damage to the unit in range with the highest HP percentage

enemy_1272_nhtank.name: Sarkaz Wither Tank
enemy_1272_nhtank.desc: Equipment of the Nachzehrer army, clearing up obstacles for the army's march. It is a powerful siege weapon, but its main role is to spread corruption.
enemy_1272_nhtank.ability:
Only attacks ground units
Melee attacks deal more damage
After several attacks, the next attack will release Corruption

enemy_1270_nhstlk.name: Rotchaser
enemy_1270_nhstlk.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Fast-moving, they do not understand the concept of healing. They exist only to create death, and the power of healing robs them of their powers.
enemy_1270_nhstlk.ability: Attacks cause the target to take Arts damage over time; effect removed when the target is healed

enemy_1093_ccsbr.name: Tiacauh Warrior
enemy_1093_ccsbr.desc: One of Acahualla's most commonly-seen warriors who uses a crude spear to attack. May pose unexpected difficulties if fighting at close range.

enemy_1093_ccsbr_2.name: Tiacauh Guard
enemy_1093_ccsbr_2.desc: A braver, fiercer warrior who uses a crude spear to attack. Years of training have given these warriors tough physiques, and they fear not even the fiercest attacks.

enemy_1095_ccripr.name: Tiacauh Fanatic
enemy_1095_ccripr.desc: A fervent Tiacauh warrior with very fast attack speed. Though each strike is not particularly powerful, those caught off guard will be quickly subdued by a rain of blows.
enemy_1095_ccripr.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1095_ccripr_2.name: Tiacauh Ripper
enemy_1095_ccripr_2.desc: An extremely fervent Tiacauh warrior with very fast attack speed. Each strike now deals noteworthy damage, and those caught off guard will be quickly subdued by a rain of blows.
enemy_1095_ccripr_2.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1096_ccwitch.name: Tiacauh Ritualist
enemy_1096_ccwitch.desc: The very few Originium Arts users in Acahualla will often serve as doctors for their tribe. Despite not developing their Originium Arts, their medical experience lets them strike where it hurts.
enemy_1096_ccwitch.ability: Gains DEF when blocked, and deals Physical damage with melee attacks.

enemy_1096_ccwitch_2.name: Tiacauh Shaman
enemy_1096_ccwitch_2.desc: These extremely rare Arts users from Acahualla have a deeper understanding of Originium Arts. With more threatening Arts in their arsenal and years of medical experience, they are a force to be reckoned with if allowed to approach up-close.
enemy_1096_ccwitch_2.ability: Gains DEF when blocked, and deals high Physical damage with melee attacks

enemy_1084_sotidm.name: Sarkaz Guerrilla Fighter
enemy_1084_sotidm.desc: A Sarkaz fighter who supports Patriot but cannot understand the special terminology of their guerilla operations. The Sarkaz ritual maintained by the Sarkaz Originium Altars will not damage them, instead strengthening their bodies and converting their attacks into Arts damage.
enemy_1084_sotidm.ability: When coming into contact with a Pulse Wave, this unit instead takes no damage and will deal Arts damage with normal attacks.

enemy_1083_sotiab.name: Guerrilla Siegebreaker
enemy_1083_sotiab.desc: One of Patriot's elite assault fighters. These troops specialize in assaults and airborne operations into enemy backlines. They wear medium armor and boast very strong individual combat capabilities. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have greatly increased attack power.
enemy_1083_sotiab.ability:
Able to land directly onto the battlefield.
When Inspired, gains greatly increased ATK.

enemy_1013_airdrp.name: Airborne Soldier
enemy_1013_airdrp.desc: One of Reunion's special melee soldiers. They are equipped with modified disposable jetpacks made from looted parts. Despite their fairly rudimentary equipment, Reunion uses them to great effect when attacking insufficiently protected areas.
enemy_1013_airdrp.ability: Able to land directly onto the battlefield.

enemy_1025_reveng_2.name: Hateful Avenger
enemy_1025_reveng_2.desc: An unidentified combatant who attacks using unconventional methods, more dangerous than a normal Avenger. Equipped with a special weapon, they are known to change their attack patterns during combat, making them difficult to defend against. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1025_reveng_2.ability: Gains significantly increased ATK when HP is under 50%

enemy_3003_alymot.name: Competition Transport Vessel
enemy_3003_alymot.desc: A transport vessel carrying supplies for setting up the competition venues. All competitors have the duty of protecting it from attacks.
enemy_3003_alymot.ability:
Enemies prioritize attacking this unit, unit will deplete Life Points upon dying
Does not count towards the defeated enemies counter

enemy_1164_bmbmot.name: Unmanned Explosive Boat
enemy_1164_bmbmot.desc: An overloaded unmanned explosive boat. Causes Corrosion after exploding
enemy_1164_bmbmot.ability: Death deals Physical damage and Corrosion to surrounding units

enemy_1162_magmot.name: Caster Speedboat
enemy_1162_magmot.desc: A Tidal Caster on a speedboat. The Caster will continue battle after the vehicle is destroyed.
enemy_1162_magmot.ability:
Attacks deal damage to the target and all allied units in the target's four adjacent tiles, additionally inflicting Corrosion
The  will continue battle after the vehicle is destroyed

enemy_1161_tidmag.name: Tidal Caster
enemy_1161_tidmag.desc: Professionally trained casters whose duties involve simulating the tides of Dossoles's sea. Adept at using Originium Arts that mimic the erosive effect of the tides.
enemy_1161_tidmag.ability: Attacks deal damage to the target and all allied units in the target's four adjacent tiles, additionally inflicting Corrosion

enemy_1159_swfmob_2.name: Delinquent Boss
enemy_1159_swfmob_2.desc: The favored get-up of pretentious delinquents. They intermingle with tourists, ready to join in a fight at any time. Better at fighting than regular delinquents, they will instinctively dodge ranged Physical attacks.
enemy_1159_swfmob_2.ability: Has a chance to dodge Physical attacks when not blocked

enemy_1160_hvyslr.name: Dockworker
enemy_1160_hvyslr.desc: Brawny brutes who work at the docks or on Pancho's ships. They enjoy playing cards and drinking, and usually spend the day in a drunken haze. Because they regularly work around seawater, they're more subjected to Erosion.
enemy_1160_hvyslr.ability:
Stuns allied units after several attacks
Quickly loses HP while under the effects of 'Erosion'

enemy_2045_smdrn.name: Collapsed Construct
enemy_2045_smdrn.desc: A drone corrupted by demonic power, now acting like a real lifeform.

enemy_1072_dlancer.name: Sarkaz Lancer
enemy_1072_dlancer.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary. After special training, they are capable of rushing forward with a long spear, building up speed. The faster they charge, the harder they strike anyone who might stand in their way.
enemy_1072_dlancer.ability: Gradually accelerates when moving, and deals additional damage on the first attack after being blocked based upon Movement Speed.

enemy_1072_dlancer_2.name: Sarkaz Lancer Leader
enemy_1072_dlancer_2.desc: A Sarkaz, more threatening than a normal Sarkaz Lancer. After special training, they are capable of rushing forward with a long spear, building up speed. The faster they charge, the harder they strike anyone who might stand in their way.
enemy_1072_dlancer_2.ability: Gradually accelerates when moving, and deals additional damage on the first attack after being blocked based upon Movement Speed.

enemy_1279_sgbdg.name: Famiglia Intimidator
enemy_1279_sgbdg.desc: Famiglia-trained strongmen, their imposing statures a bluff of sorts. In actuality, famiglie value their mastery of Siracusan language over raw violence.
enemy_1279_sgbdg.ability:
More likely to be attacked by allied units
Increases Blood Debt when defeated

enemy_1071_dftman_2.name: Sarkaz Blademaster
enemy_1071_dftman_2.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary, more skilled than an ordinary Sarkaz Swordsman. Though scarred by years of war, they are still fighting to fulfill the orders of their employers.

enemy_2046_smwar.name: Shattered Champion
enemy_2046_smwar.desc: Once an honorable warrior of the Treescar tribe, now a part of the demons' vanguard, together with their fallen chief.

enemy_1076_bsthmr.name: Demolitionist
enemy_1076_bsthmr.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.name: Demolitionist Leader
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear, more aggressive than normal Demolitionists. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr_2.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1352_eslime.name: Radioactive Originium Slug
enemy_1352_eslime.desc: A wild Infected creature that accidentally consumed too much industrial waste, causing it to leak Arts energy upon death. These low-intelligence creatures are easily manipulated by enemy casters to launch swarming attacks on manmade facilities.
enemy_1352_eslime.ability: When defeated, deals heavy Arts damage in an area and causes all enemies on the field to gain 1 Energy.

enemy_1357_erob.name: Paramilitary Axeman
enemy_1357_erob.desc: An outlaw hired to sow chaos, equipped with a thermal energy axe that consumes too much power. The energy supply isn't quite sufficient to start it up.
enemy_1357_erob.ability:
Energy Capacity: 20; Gains [Overload] upon reaching max Energy.
Continuously drains 1 Energy from all nearby ground enemies.
[Overload] Greatly increases ASPD, DEF, and RES for a longer period of time.

enemy_1356_egun.name: Paramilitary Sniper
enemy_1356_egun.desc: An outlaw hired to sow chaos, equipped with a thermal energy blaster with its output cranked up too high. However, as a result of this, they are able to charge the equipment of other gangsters.
enemy_1356_egun.ability:
Energy Capacity: 20; Gains [Overload] upon reaching max Energy.
Periodically stops moving and continuously provides Energy to all enemies within attack range.
[Overload] Greatly increases ASPD, DEF, and RES for a longer period of time.

enemy_1046_agent_2.name: Infantry Leader
enemy_1046_agent_2.desc: A basic melee soldier. They are stronger than normal Infantry.

enemy_1049_eagent_2.name: Agent Leader
enemy_1049_eagent_2.desc: A caster who utilizes coordinated tactics. They are stronger than normal Agents. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with Arts damage, and employ a variety of techniques to break through unprepared defenders.
enemy_1049_eagent_2.ability: Attacks temporarily reduce allied units' Block

enemy_1047_sagent_2.name: Sniper Leader
enemy_1047_sagent_2.desc: A basic ranged soldier. They are stronger than normal Snipers, firing improved ammunition that stuns for a longer duration. Proceed with caution.
enemy_1047_sagent_2.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1058_traink_2.name: Bulldozer
enemy_1058_traink_2.desc: Special melee combatants. Their unstoppable bulk is equivalent to blocking three normal enemies, and Operators who cannot block three enemies may not be able to halt their advance at all. Compared to a normal Bully, they boast greater attack power and armor.
enemy_1058_traink_2.ability: Can only be blocked by Operators with 3 or more Block

enemy_1059_buster_2.name: Desperate Fanatic
enemy_1059_buster_2.desc: Special long-range combatants, with greater combat capabilities than normal Fanatics. Wielding a massive iron hammer, they boast enormous destructive potential and will targeted attacking facilities first. They can also hit Operators stationed on high ground.

enemy_1250_lyengs.name: Rhine Engineering Member
enemy_1250_lyengs.desc: An engineer of Rhine Lab. Responsible for both providing equipment and giving their professional opinion on its production, they also maintain a close relationship with the Energy Section.

enemy_1251_lysyta.name: Dor-1 Failure
enemy_1251_lysyta.desc: A product of Dorothy's failed experiments. An irregularly-shaped silver object emanating a soft glow, like a slumbering Dreamland.
enemy_1251_lysyta.ability: When touching an inactive , the Power Armor recovers some SP

enemy_1253_lysytc.name: Dor-3 Bionic
enemy_1253_lysytc.desc: A product of Dorothy's private experiments. To her, it is not a mere creation, it is her family, her dream.
enemy_1253_lysytc.ability:
When not blocked, Physical and Arts damage taken is significantly reduced
When touching an inactive , the Power Armor recovers a lot of SP

enemy_1255_lybgpa.name: R-31 Heavy Power Armor
enemy_1255_lybgpa.desc: A model that appeared around half a year ago among some mercenary groups, its performance is on par with mass-produced military models from five years ago. Rhine Lab's Energy Section cooperates with many parties, and continues to look for ever more opportunities.
enemy_1255_lybgpa.ability: [Overload] Continuously deals Physical damage to the target proportional to the unit's ATK, andStuns the target

enemy_1256_lyacpa.name: R-11 Assault Power Armor
enemy_1256_lyacpa.desc: A model equipped with long-ranged weapons, massively increasing its offensive capabilities. While these experimental weapons are still in the prototype phase, its energy and firepower cannot be overlooked.

enemy_1042_frostd.name: Frost
enemy_1042_frostd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone equipped with heavy armor to make it harder to destroy. Its prototype was designed by a graphics engine studio. They are equipped with a special device that allows them to drastically reduce the temperature around them, greatly decreasing the attack speed of nearby Operators.
enemy_1042_frostd.ability:
Flying unit.
All friendly units within range have reduced ASPD

enemy_1311_mhkryk.name: Cautelotaur
enemy_1311_mhkryk.desc: A predator crafty by nature, with sightings of this species rarely reported. After feeding on the Chitotex Clawbeast, it uses the leftover carapace as a defensive tool, blocking fatal attacks in fights.
enemy_1311_mhkryk.ability: After feeding on a  or , keeps its shell to use as a defensive tool, significantly lowering all damage except to the rear

enemy_1311_mhkryk_2.name: Kouaku Cautelotaur
enemy_1311_mhkryk_2.desc: A cunning and ruthless variety of Cautelotaur, it conceals itself in herds to launch surprise attacks, the prey it catches subjected to its capricious whims. Most hunters that attempt to hunt this creature return with nothing to show for it.
enemy_1311_mhkryk_2.ability: After feeding on a  or , keeps its shell to use as a defensive tool, significantly lowering all damage except to the rear

enemy_1306_mhtrtl.name: Chitotex Clawbeast
enemy_1306_mhtrtl.desc: An herbivore that dwells by cliffsides, their front claws equipped for digging out rock faces. They grow rear shells hard enough to withstand falling stones, but bear unusually soft bellies. Their delicious meat is the primary cause of their falling population.
enemy_1306_mhtrtl.ability: Weak spot located on its front, damage dealt to the rear is significantly decreased

enemy_1306_mhtrtl_2.name: Keratex Clawbeast
enemy_1306_mhtrtl_2.desc: A Chitotex Clawbeast subspecies particular to certain regions. Their sturdy upper limbs accelerate their excavation of rock exponentially, while their stern shells can shrug off severe batterings. These traits allow them to better expand their territory.
enemy_1306_mhtrtl_2.ability: Weak spot located on its front, damage dealt to the rear is significantly decreased

enemy_1304_mhwolf.name: Oxmane
enemy_1304_mhwolf.desc: A carnivore common to the mountains that hunts primarily in packs. Bearing developed mandibles, they crush their prey's skulls with ease. Most fond of feeding on hornbeast stragglers.

enemy_1307_mhrhcr_2.name: Gravorobeast
enemy_1307_mhrhcr_2.desc: A Basorobeast subspecies particular to certain regions. Regimens of knocking into cliff rock since infancy have honed their heads to exceptional sturdiness, able to shrug off fierce frontal attacks. Survival never ends, and the struggle never ceases.
enemy_1307_mhrhcr_2.ability: Weak spot located on its rear, damage dealt to the front is significantly decreased

enemy_1309_mhboar_2.name: Raging Direswine
enemy_1309_mhboar_2.desc: A variety of Direswine extremely easy to anger, they dominate areas near water sources with great swagger. If one is enraged, it will indiscriminately rampage about with a madness sufficient to lay anything in the vicinity low.
enemy_1309_mhboar_2.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 2 Block
Gradually accelerates when charging, dealing damage based on Movement Speed to any target it passes through or is blocked by

enemy_1310_mhprpn_2.name: Rainer Haribeast
enemy_1310_mhprpn_2.desc: A variety of haribeast growing unusual thorns, which can be shot at speeds rivaling that of arrows. Minute barbs grow on the tips, leaving wounds that fail to heal long after the skin is broken. Once, someone announced they'd successfully tamed one of these creatures; afterwards, they were never seen again.
enemy_1310_mhprpn_2.ability:
Weak spot located on its front, damage dealt to the rear is decreased
Deals damage in a semicircle to the rear at every fixed portion of health lost

enemy_1274_stmram.name: Steam Tank
enemy_1274_stmram.desc: Armored vehicle employing Victorian steam technology, even more dangerous when it has taken damage.
enemy_1274_stmram.ability: When HP drops to 50%, ASPD and Movement Speed are greatly increased, and becomes immune to most control effects.

enemy_1271_nhkodo.name: Sarkaz Wither Devourer
enemy_1271_nhkodo.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. Devours any obstacle in its way, including the living. Anything and everything is food to it.
enemy_1271_nhkodo.ability:
When blocked for the first time, devours the blocker. DEF is greatly increased, but Movement Speed is reduced
Returns devoured Operator when defeated

enemy_1080_sotidp_2.name: Guerrilla Herald Leader
enemy_1080_sotidp_2.desc: Compared to regular heralds, these leaders have greater individual combat capabilities. The heralds are important links within their communication system. While present, they will issue tactical commands to increase the offensive and defensive abilities of nearby allies.
enemy_1080_sotidp_2.ability: Increases the ATK and DEF of enemy units when on the battlefield.

enemy_1114_rgrdmn.name: Sarkaz Centurion Host
enemy_1114_rgrdmn.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary unwittingly converted into one of Mephisto's Herd, able to damage multiple targets at once and drain vitality from its victims. Prevents others from entering the Sarcophagus - do not push too far in, and you may yet avoid their wrath.
enemy_1114_rgrdmn.ability:
Attacks 3 enemies simultaneously
Restores a certain amount of HP after each attack, based upon damage dealt

enemy_1114_rgrdmn_2.name: Sarkaz Centurion Nidus Guard
enemy_1114_rgrdmn_2.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary leader unwittingly converted into one of Mephisto's Herd, able to damage multiple targets at once and drain vitality from its victims. More ferociously prevents others from entering the Sarcophagus.
enemy_1114_rgrdmn_2.ability:
Attacks 3 enemies simultaneously
Restores a certain amount of HP after each attack, based upon damage dealt

enemy_1140_merhd_2.name: Tactical Hound Pro
enemy_1140_merhd_2.desc: An infected creature controlled by a Caster. Having gone through rigorous training, they now pose a much greater threat. Hounds are widely used by mercenaries, though some Perros object to it.

enemy_1141_merbow_2.name: Tactical Crossbowman Leader
enemy_1141_merbow_2.desc: A mercenary using ranged weaponry. Uses a crossbow in battle and calls for the more powerful offensive Air Support Drone 'Scrap Model-U' to carry out aerial bombardment.
enemy_1141_merbow_2.ability: Before the first attack, calls for  to support the battle from the Mobile Tactical Hangar

enemy_1142_mershd_2.name: Tactical Defender Leader
enemy_1142_mershd_2.desc: A mercenary clad in heavy armor. Has even stronger defensive capabilities and utilizes the more powerful Air Support Drone 'Anvil Model-B' to provide defensive support to their allies.
enemy_1142_mershd_2.ability: The first time HP drops below half, calls for  to support the battle from the Mobile Tactical Hangar

enemy_1145_atkspd_2.name: Scrap Model-U
enemy_1145_atkspd_2.desc: The enemy's advanced offensive Air Support Drones. Its attacks deal stronger AoE Physical damage and reduce the targets' DEF.
enemy_1145_atkspd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Attacks fire two grenades, significantly reducing the DEF of the target and nearby allies for a short period
Difficult to attack
Will automatically retreat some time after launch

enemy_1146_defspd_2.name: Anvil Model-B
enemy_1146_defspd_2.desc: The enemy's advanced defensive Air Support Drones. It greatly strengthens the DEF of enemy units within range and gradually recovers their HP.
enemy_1146_defspd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Continually provides boosted DEF and HP recovery to nearby enemy units
Difficult to attack
Will automatically retreat some time after launch

enemy_1212_mtrfol.name: Pathfinder Legionarius
enemy_1212_mtrfol.desc: A warrior who has flocked to the Guide, inspired by his philosophy. Fights to bring the common goal of the Pathfinders to fruition.

enemy_1215_ptrarc.name: Pathfinder Veles
enemy_1215_ptrarc.desc: A warrior who specializes in dropping into battle from the skies. They take greater pleasure from seeing their foes flustered by the loss of their supplies than actually defeating them.
enemy_1215_ptrarc.ability:
Able to land directly onto the battlefield.
Prioritizes attacking 'Gelato Stops'. Deals additional Arts Damage to 'Gelato Stops'.

enemy_1216_cansld.name: Pathfinder Ballistarius
enemy_1216_cansld.desc: A stalwart warrior who stands on the front lines, firing shells capable of inflicting widespread destruction. By virtue of their physique, they can withstand the recoil from their cannons, as well as hold their own in close combat even without their weapons. Extremely reliable.
enemy_1216_cansld.ability:
While this unit is holding Ammo, attacks become long-ranged and deal Splash Damage. Ammo capacity: 2
Deals additional Arts damage to 'Gelato Stops'

enemy_1112_emppnt.name: Imperial Artillery Targeteer
enemy_1112_emppnt.desc: A heavily-armored, sturdily-built drone operated by artillery casters of the Ursus army. They presage the withering firepower of the Ursus Artillery.

enemy_1112_emppnt_2.name: Imperial Artillery Core Targeteer
enemy_1112_emppnt_2.desc: A heavily-armored, sturdily-built core drone operated by artillery technicians of the Ursus army. They presage the withering firepower of the Ursus Artillery. The Ursus Artillery is a fire team that has bunkered down into the city. Though packing less firepower than naval guns, their equipment still shreds through buildings and armor with ease.

enemy_1020_obsv.name: Technical Scout
enemy_1020_obsv.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. They are able to utilize devices to assess the status of Operators and their equipment. Prioritize dealing with them.
enemy_1020_obsv.ability: Reveals invisible allied units in its surroundings

enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.name: Nova Knightclub Elite
enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.desc: An elite of the Nova Knightclub. With the Candle Knight as its figurehead, this Knightclub with a beautiful name recruits elegant knights proficient in using Arts from far and wide. Many of these elites have achieved great mastery over their Arts, some even plotting to overthrow the Candle Knight.
enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.ability:
[Glimmering Touch] After charging for some time, deals a large amount of Arts damage to a target and all surrounding units
While charging, loses Invisibility

enemy_1067_snslime_2.name: Infused Glacial Originium Slug α
enemy_1067_snslime_2.desc: An infected wild creature native to colder environments that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen. More threatening than normal Infused Glacial Originium Slugs, they explode upon death, damaging and freezing nearby Operators. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by Yeti Squadron members for various tactical purposes.
enemy_1067_snslime_2.ability: Upon death, deals Physical damage in an area and applies Cold to affected targets

enemy_1064_snsbr.name: Yeti Operative
enemy_1064_snsbr.desc: A basic melee combatants of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. They are skilled at attacking frozen Operators.
enemy_1064_snsbr.ability: Attack power increases when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1069_icebrk_2.name: Yeti Icecleaver Leader
enemy_1069_icebrk_2.desc: A special melee combatant of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected gerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. Experienced executioners on the battlefield, they wield long katanas that can easily ravage frozen Operators, and their nickname, 'Ice Cleaver,' references their devastating blows that seem strong enough to split glaciers.
enemy_1069_icebrk_2.ability: Attack power increases greatly when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1068_snmage_2.name: Yeti Caster Leader
enemy_1068_snmage_2.desc: An elite caster of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra, boasting greater combat capabilities. Through special Arts and devices, they can inflict Cold on our Operators, reducing their attack speed and making them vulnerable to being Frozen.
enemy_1068_snmage_2.ability: After two attacks, the next attack applies the Cold effect

enemy_1242_ltsmag_2.name: Remnant Principal Violinist
enemy_1242_ltsmag_2.desc: Scheming remnants of the Witch King, they were once the principal violinist of a certain orchestra in the capital. It's said that they stole their position from another by forcing the stage setup to be changed.
enemy_1242_ltsmag_2.ability: Attacks can travel along Realigned Flux, dealing damage to all connected units

enemy_1240_ltgint_2.name: Enhanced Mass-Produced Colossus
enemy_1240_ltgint_2.desc: An Originium Arts creation that sacrifices stability for miniaturization. Very well-equipped for breaching, it is often used to break through enemy lines. Its physical strength has been improved but its weakness to interference remains an issue.
enemy_1240_ltgint_2.ability:
Can only be blocked by units with at least 2 Block
When in Realigned Flux, DEF and RES are greatly reduced

enemy_1241_ltmage_2.name: Battleworn Spire Caster
enemy_1241_ltmage_2.desc: A Spire Caster with experience in war. Specializes in causing extreme area damage under relatively stable conditions. While highly confident, they are still vulnerable to interference from external energy.
enemy_1241_ltmage_2.ability:
Attacks 2 enemies simultaneously
When attacking targets outside of Realigned Flux, deals Splash damage

enemy_1321_wdarft.name: Withering Collector Disciple
enemy_1321_wdarft.desc: An organic construct containing the Nachzehrer King's Originium Arts. It can extract all kinds of dead souls from the battlefield to create Seeds of Withering.

enemy_1321_wdarft_2.name: Decaying Collector Disciple
enemy_1321_wdarft_2.desc: An organic construct containing the Nachzehrer King's Originium Arts. It can extract all kinds of dead souls from the battlefield to create Seeds of Decay.

enemy_1269_nhfly.name: Seed of Withering
enemy_1269_nhfly.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Mindless except for an obsession with self-destruction. They create death for others to feed upon.
enemy_1269_nhfly.ability:
Flying unit.
Self-destructs when near an Operator, dealing Physical damage

enemy_1269_nhfly_2.name: Seed of Decay
enemy_1269_nhfly_2.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. It is a violation of the laws of nature, rejecting life for death. They create death for others to feed upon.
enemy_1269_nhfly_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Self-destructs when near an Operator, dealing Physical damage

enemy_1266_nhapos.name: Rot Eater
enemy_1266_nhapos.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. The longer they remain on the battlefield, the harder they are to defeat, absorbing the enemy's strength as they grow increasingly deadly.
enemy_1266_nhapos.ability:
The more enemies defeated, the higher its HP, ATK, and DEF when spawned from the Twigs That Crave
Releases Corruption, causing your nearby units to continuously lose HP

enemy_1005_yokai_3.name: Raptor
enemy_1005_yokai_3.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, its design and name seem to be inspired by a certain space game. Equipped with a minigun and a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Their outstanding stealthiness and attack power pose a major threat to Operators.

enemy_1005_yokai.name: Monster
enemy_1005_yokai.desc: An unmanned enemy drone, called 'Monster' by Reunion forces. Equipped with a long-range Arts receiver, they are likely operated by concealed Casters. Though unlikely to cause heavy damage to armored Operators, they can still pose a considerable threat.

enemy_1040_bombd.name: Bombtail
enemy_1040_bombd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that flies slowly. They carry an explosive warhead that is launched when approaching nearby Operators, inflicting physical damage in an area. Their design seems to have been inspired by a fantasy creature. Though they cannot attack anymore after launching the warhead, their movement speed increases afterwards due to the loss of weight.
enemy_1040_bombd.ability:
Flying unit.
Can launch a warhead to deal AoE physical damage
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after launching warhead

enemy_1041_lazerd_2.name: Arts Master A2
enemy_1041_lazerd_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that had some of its Flight Support modules swapped out, trading speed for a more powerful Arts Weapon module. Capable of using long-range Arts attacks. Proceed with caution. Based on available data, its weapon module also allows it to block some Arts damage.

enemy_1062_rager.name: Enraged Possessed Soldier
enemy_1062_rager.desc: An enemy combatant who has slowly descended into primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, and they are unusually aggressive as a result. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1062_rager.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1063_rageth.name: Enraged Possessed Thrower
enemy_1063_rageth.desc: An enemy ranged combatant who has slowly descended into primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, and they will hurl whatever they can get their hands on at their targets. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1063_rageth.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1063_rageth_2.name: Enraged Possessed Bonethrower
enemy_1063_rageth_2.desc: An enemy ranged combatant who has completely given in to primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, they will even rip out the Originium lodged inside their bodies to throw because of their deadened sense of pain. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1063_rageth_2.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1174_duholy_2.name: Dublinn Elite Companion Guard
enemy_1174_duholy_2.desc: A member of the Dublinn forces' elite operations squad, they are much stronger than regular squad members. The shields they wield are the culmination of many nations' technologies, providing cover for their partners while advancing, and protecting all nearby allies from harm.
enemy_1174_duholy_2.ability:
More likely to get attacked by your units
When a  is nearby, activates a field that reduces nearby allied units' ASPD
Refraction

enemy_1175_dushdo_2.name: Dublinn Elite Companion Shadowblade
enemy_1175_dushdo_2.desc: A member of the Dublinn forces' elite operations squad, they are much stronger than regular squad members. When they move alongside their partners, they gain powerful stealth capabilities and their attacks are much swifter.
enemy_1175_dushdo_2.ability:
Invisible
When a  is nearby, significantly increases ASPD

enemy_1092_mdgint.name: Mudrock Colossus
enemy_1092_mdgint.desc: This Originium construct, crafted by Mudrock, does not possess sentience and must be directly controlled by Mudrock. Though bulky and slow, they are extremely durable and boast terrifying physical damage and survivability.

enemy_1172_dugago_2.name: Enraged Tombkeeper Grotesque
enemy_1172_dugago_2.desc: A special Arts creation from the Dublinn forces, created by their leader Mandragora. According to Mandragora, back when she was studying Originium Arts in an outskirts cemetery, the Tombkeeper Grotesque was her first companion. Its outer appearance has gone through many redesigns, with the latest being based off a Sarkaz from an ancient bloodline. This series of Tombkeepers embodies Mandragora's fury towards Victoria.
enemy_1172_dugago_2.ability:
After first defeat, temporarily transforms into a stone statue with extremely high DEF, then revives as an aerial unit
Refraction

enemy_1288_duskls_2.name: Dublinn Flamechaser Elite Soldier
enemy_1288_duskls_2.desc: A elite Dublinn soldier fused with their Leader's ember. Chasing their Leader's ember even more fervently, their bodies are manipulated by Arts to ceaselessly rise from the dead.
enemy_1288_duskls_2.ability:
Attacks deal additional Burn while near burning Reeds
When defeated, turns into an Invisible  that must be damaged 5 times to defeat; respawns after a period of time

enemy_1289_dubmb_2.name: Dublinn Cannoneer Captain
enemy_1289_dubmb_2.desc: Captain of a ranged squad in the Dublinn forces. They use a modified standard-issue Victorian military crossbow fitted with a launcher for even more powerful explosive ammunition.
enemy_1289_dubmb_2.ability: After two attacks, the next attack deals Splash damage

enemy_1292_duskld_2.name: Dublinn Flamechaser Elite Guard
enemy_1292_duskld_2.desc: An elite Dublinn defender fused with their Leader's ember. Chasing their Leader's ember even more fervently, the Arts embedded in their equipment make them hard to defeat.
enemy_1292_duskld_2.ability:
Attacks deal additional Burn and takes reduced Physical and Arts damage while near burning Reeds
When defeated, turns into an Invisible  that must be damaged 10 times to defeat; respawns after a period of time

enemy_1295_ymph_2.name: Raging Di Beast
enemy_1295_ymph_2.desc: One of Ya's off-handed creations, its bloodlust honed by the golden sands. It is extremely keen of hearing and stalks the streets like a shadow, both fighting and collecting and relaying information.

enemy_1299_ymkilr_2.name: Shanhaizhong Secret-Keeper
enemy_1299_ymkilr_2.desc: A Shanhaizhong leader with a mysterious mission. Elusive, merciless, and unconcerned with their men's sacrifices. What are they really planning behind that fog of faith?
enemy_1299_ymkilr_2.ability:
Invisible
The next attack made after breaking Invisibility will deal increased damage

enemy_1300_ymmir_2.name: Shanhaizhong Oracle
enemy_1300_ymmir_2.desc: Shanhaizhong members who can cast strange Arts. They superstitiously believe in those huge beings and resent their own compatriots, shouting 'the mounts, seas, and all in between shall be our master's' while displaying a 'divine power' of unknown origin.
enemy_1300_ymmir_2.ability: Continuously locks on to a target and inflicts a large burst of Arts damage after the channel completes, gaining increased ASPD afterwards

enemy_1254_lypa.name: Experimental Power Armor
enemy_1254_lypa.desc: An unpiloted Power Armor. It is able to function without a pilot, but nevertheless maintains high agility.

enemy_1264_durgrd_2.name: Whiskey-Grade Waker-Upper
enemy_1264_durgrd_2.desc: A terrifying machine that sweeps across the beach, equipped with an ultra-powerful water cannon strong enough to blast any drunkard to their senses. After someone secretly tampered with its targeting logic, it now only blasts sober people.
enemy_1264_durgrd_2.ability: Upon taking damage for the first time, gains significantly increased ASPD and Movement Speed for a period of time

enemy_1331_cbsisy.name: 'Crossroads' Production Model
enemy_1331_cbsisy.desc: Unmanned defense vehicles primarily developed by Volvort Kochinski. They emit mid-length lasers in all four directions, providing excellent defense in narrow corridors, and significantly hindering the enemy even in open terrain.
enemy_1331_cbsisy.ability:
Attacks in 4 directions while moving, dealing Arts damage and deals increased damage to nearby targets
Continuously tries to push nearby

enemy_1062_rager_2.name: Enraged Possessed Leader
enemy_1062_rager_2.desc: An enemy combatant who has completely given in to primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, and their death-defying aggression breaks the bounds of reason. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1062_rager_2.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1267_nhpbr_2.name: Sarkaz Witherer Captain
