endings.name: To Talk with the Mountains
endings.desc: The mountains have been crossed, and the expeditioners' tracks left all over the endless icefield. The split earth talks to you, questions you, promises you. The quest for knowledge has no end, and misfortune is only the beginning.
cond: Default ending. Clear the floor 5 Dreadful Foe 「Lofty Silverfrost」 (or 「Ice-Cold Image」 on difficulty 12+) while holding neither 「Boundless Gift」 nor 「Dimensional Fluid」.
The second-to-last node of floor 5 is always a Prophecy ('Past the Arm of Sami').

endings.name: Winterfall
endings.desc: Fate arrives at his end, lowering its curtain. The warrior's last drop of blood freezes to ice before it can be shed.
endings.change: The fortress built in the north has granted you passage, and the warriors will scale the high walls and watch the borders of Sami day and night.
cond: First achieve the ending 「To Talk with the Mountains」 to unlock the related choices and rune tablets.
At a Lost and Found node, pick the option that adds +4 Collapse Value to obtain the two rune tablets 「Void」 and 「Scar」.

Before the third-to-last node of floor 5, invoke the 「Void」+「Scar」 rune verdict once and the node to the right becomes a Prophecy ('Omen of the Tree Fissure') — choose 'Search the camp' to obtain 「Treescar Helm」.

In 'Past the Arm of Sami', choosing to don the helm changes the floor 5 Dreadful Foe to 「Sami's Entropy」 (「Hollow Visage」 on difficulty 12+) and the final boss to 「'Eikthyrnir,' the Scar of the Shattered」.
Achieved by clearing floor 5 while holding neither 「Boundless Gift」 nor 「Dimensional Fluid」.

endings.name: Long into an Abyss
endings.desc: You have seen the impenetrable depths of darkness, and have felt a certain sheet of heavy snow fall onto your shoulders. Now there is only silence.
endings.change: The vision of the Cyclops is now shared with you. Deep in the icefields, the full picture of the prophecy is yet to be revealed.
cond: First achieve the ending 「Winterfall」 to unlock the related choices and collectibles.
At a Scout node, choose to hear the seer's opinion to obtain the collectible 「Routeweave Net」 (extra enemies appear in all regular and emergency battles outside floor 6).

The seer marks the bottom path of the next floor with an [Emergency Ops → Prophecy] layout; that Prophecy is always 'The Pessimist's Prison' — choose 'Take the altar flower' to obtain 「Boundless Gift」.
Clear floor 5 holding 「Boundless Gift」 but not 「Dimensional Fluid」 to enter floor 6, then clear the final boss 「Gardener」 (「Sentinel」 on difficulty 12+; final boss: endlessly echoing Kreshen).

endings.name: From Start To End
endings.desc: You travel through an eternity of sand and war, and find yourself back at the base of the megastructure. This signifies the end of a long journey, but also the beginning of a new one. A group of expeditioners will follow in your footsteps, setting out to explore the land beyond the giant structure, and eventually discover the truth.
endings.change: The circular gate lies within the cold winds, waiting to be opened once more.
cond: After Expansion II, achieve any one ending to unlock the related events and collectibles.
Obtain the collectible 「Horizon Invitation」 (usually sold by the Rogue Trader for just 1 Originium Ingot). Afterwards you can meet the event 'Change' at Encounter nodes.
In 'Change', choose to present the invitation to enter the battle 「황사 환경」; win it to obtain 「Dimensional Fluid」.

Clear floor 5 while holding 「Dimensional Fluid」 to enter the hidden floor 7 (¬ΔS) — then clear the final boss 「Sands of Time」 (「Enter Eternity」 on difficulty 12+; final bosses: the King of Calendars and the Scourge of the Land).
If you obtained 「Dimensional Fluid」 on the hidden floor 6, you must clear floor 6 first to enter floor 7.

endings.name: The Fleetest of Light
endings.desc: An incorrect deduction, a mistaken responsibility, an unnecessary sacrifice. You have found a way here for those who come afterwards, a secret that fate shall keep.
endings.change: Unrealized changes detailed in this ending
cond: Deep Investigation-only ending. Clearing the last Dreadful Foe battle in Deep Investigation always grants 「The Fleetest of Light」 (no ending icon). Prophecy nodes do not appear in Deep Investigation, and floor 6 cannot be entered.

encounters.title: Sobbing in the Swamp
encounters.desc: You lead your team forward with caution, afraid of running into some strange Sami entity. But before long you hear something sobbing by your ears. You quickly alert your team members to prepare for battle, but a long period of time passes with no terrifying enemies showing up. You finally muster the courage to head towards the source of the sound, and realize that it is a Perro sobbing nonstop.

choices.title: You must be doing this on purpose!

next.desc: You tell him to hurry up and leave, but he just stays there squirming back and forth the place as he sobs. This annoys you to the point where you finally prepare to forcefully kick him out.

choices.title: Want some candy?
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but gain 2 Objective Shield

next.desc: You're angry over the fact that he spent half a day hiding behind a tree without saying a word to you, but at the very least he's just a person and not some frightening Sami monster. You let your team members rest there for a while before continuing onward.

encounters.title: A Profitable Plan
encounters.desc: You've never doubted the Duck Lord's ability to cause trouble, but still you feel a chill creeping up your spine at the sight of him gathering a bunch of tainted Sami people who are conducting a rite. Even though he tells you that these things are just tools for creating wealth, and they haven't attacked you of their own volition, this event itself is just too bizarre!

next.desc: You don't have a clue what this rite is about, but one thing is clear: you can't let these tainted Sami people continue to exist on Sami land. On top of that, you also have to teach this maverick duck a lesson.
There are so many strange things in Sami, so you can turn a blind eye to the ones that aren't actually a threat.

encounters.title: Entourage
encounters.desc: You see a luxurious motorcade parked in Sami. The people in the cars seem rich and powerful, but there don't seem to be any bodyguards or mercenaries around. You inadvertently catch a glimpse of the Duck Lord chatting in one of these cars, and when you scan the area again, you spot Gopnik leaning against a tree.

next.desc: Clearly it was the Duck Lord who duped all these people and brought them to this dangerous place. You'll use your fists to drive them back to Columbia. Oh, Gopnik's spotted you.

choices.title: Eavesdrop on their conversation
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Points, but gain 3 Hope

choices.title: We should just leave
choices.desc: What's that got to do with me?

next.desc: Even if they were to reveal some Terra-shattering news, you have no way to cash in on that right now, so let's just forget about it.

encounters.title: Duck Lord's Sinister Plot
encounters.desc: Just like in those Columbian comics, in the remote uninhabited wilderness sits a villain's fortress. He and his minion lurk within, secretly hatching a plan to develop terrifying weapons that will threaten the entire land... Oh, this villain... is a duck?

choices.title: He's definitely up to no good!

next.desc: You know something is up when you see the Duck Lord show his face, and you prepare to strike before they can even react.

choices.title: Leave
choices.desc: What's that got to do with me?

next.desc: Your mission to explore is a serious one, and you're not about to waste any more time here getting involved in the Duck Lord's nefarious plans. After all, should anything happen, surely the Columbian government would sort it out, right?

encounters.title: Chain of Camps
encounters.desc: There seems to be a sizeable army stationed on the icefield, camps stretched out as far as the eye can see. Is it Ursus? Higashi? Or even Yan? The style of the barracks doesn't match any of them, so it might be none of those nations. Perhaps you should take a closer look.

next.desc: Arrows shot from the camp deals some damage to your party, but when you walk into the camp you find nothing inside. Just like a phantom, a scam. As you prepare to leave, a loud horn sounds out from thin air, and Kuranta emerge from the empty phantom tent in single file, dressed in unusual clothing. They quickly line up in formation, encircling a general in the center. He points his weapon at you.

The camp is so huge it takes you a long time simpler to reach its perimeter. However, you're surprised to find several other camps of similar size connected end to end, just like a lone knight leaving his footprints on the earth.

The camp is so huge it takes you a long time simpler to reach its perimeter. However, you're surprised to find several other camps of similar size connected end to end, just like a lone knight leaving his footprints on the earth.

encounters.title: On-stage Invitation
encounters.desc: Scarlet tents are pitched on the silverwhite icefield, and those unaware of the inside story lift the curtains to find a play about to begin.

choices.title: Let's watch a little
choices.desc: Let's treat this as a break

next.desc: The play staged here is titled 'Frost Buck and Snow Doe,' the tale of a snowpriest's pair of twin guards, and the duo's tragic deaths. How distasteful... wait, why are those actors pointing their weapons at the audience...? ...Why are the backup dancers jumping off the stage? ...What are you people doing?

choices.title: Where's our wallet?
choices.desc: Lose 12 Originium Ingots, but gain a fairly uncommon Foldartal

next.desc: What kind of theater troupe would come to sparsely populated Sami to perform a play? It's probably better we stay away.

encounters.title: Northwind Witch
encounters.desc: You didn't expect to find a little cabin out in the desolate icefield, owned by a polite and proper multilingual Sarkaz lady. She invites you in for a rest and even prepares tea and desserts for you. Afterwards, she picks up her basket and heads out into the snow to pick plants. As she leaves, she instructs you to rest in the house and not to wander around. Or you may leave when you're done resting. In short, it seems she doesn't want you poking around the house looking for the secrets she has hidden within.

choices.title: Walk around and touch the furniture freely
choices.desc: A quick look should be fine

next.desc: The house seems to be infuriated by your rudeness. It splits apart into four nachzehrer branches, and the ones resting by the fireplace... oh no, are those rotchasers?! Could this scary lady possibly be someone important in the Nachzehrer Royal Court?! No one else in their right mind would build a house with these things!

choices.title: Let's get out of here
choices.desc: Your own life is the most important

next.desc: You've heard many strange tales related to Sami, and some of scary shamans who would trick people into a house, only to have the actually living house eat them up. The more you think about it, the more frightened you become, so it's better that you get out before it's too late, before the witch does something to you.

encounters.title: Always on Alert
encounters.desc: You meet six wandering souls on the icefield, undoubtedly tainted. From their appearance, they look like regular victims of no fame. Perhaps you'll want to show mercy and set them free here.

choices.title: Make a move
choices.desc: Better to just get it over with

next.desc: You ready up your team, and a Sniper Operator takes the lead in your attack. However, the bolts they fire are casually caught by the enemy and melted in mid-air with Arts. You immediately realize these guys are not to be trifled with, but there is no turning back now.
Your intuition tells you that these guys are not easy to deal with, so you lead your team around them.

encounters.title: Chain of Suspicion
encounters.desc: A Sami tribe has taken in a large number of outsiders. However, the differences in race and nationality make them suspicious of each other, and whispered complaints form an undercurrent running through the tribe. There are no Collapsals here, but eventually the shadow of a lie shatters the people's sanity, leaving the whole tribe in disarray.

choices.title: Survey the situation while maintaining order

next.desc: You feel a completely inexplicable urge for cathartic violence rising inside you, so you quickly leave with your team members.

encounters.title: Bob's Emporium
encounters.desc: Canned vegetables, beer, spices. These products are evidently popular in this area, and you wait half a day for a chance to talk to the owner of this little store alone. You only get two words out before the other party excitedly dives into the shelves, trying to find something from the slow-moving and overstocked goods to hand you as a gift.

choices.title: Pick the item in his hand
Pick something from deep inside the warehouse
Pick something new from a trolley

encounters.title: Northwizard Cup
encounters.desc: Every clan is here! Even those who have stumbled in are welcome. After sharing a number of stories, jokes, and treesap juice, the Sami suddenly fall silent as a few men clearly dressed like snowpriests solemnly walk into the woods. The Sami host of this gathering approaches you and asks in a methodical tone if you would like to 'choose a fighter.' Behind him is a group of grotesque 'giants' being driven out of the woods by the snowpriests.

choices.title: We've seen the register, now choose your fighter!
Lose five times in a row!
choices.desc: You question time, self, and the earth in your head; gain a random Collectible

choices.title: But somehow, you feels a little... hollow
This seems time-consuming, and we have places to be
choices.desc: He bids farewell to you, and hopes you stay warm and fed

next.desc: You leave the clan gathering grounds, and from your back come the sounds of tree trunks crashing into the soil and hearty laughter of the people.

choices.title: Awesome! Exciting! But we should get going
choices.desc: This game of chance should be played in moderation

next.desc: You leave the clan gathering grounds, and from your back come the sounds of tree trunks crashing into the soil and hearty laughter of the people.

choices.title: First-timers in Sami have definitely never seen this before!
Ha! There's no way a bunch of branches could beat a boulder!
Don't judge your opponent's abilities from just their physique!
Hothands Sidan has clearly had plenty of practice with its palms!
Even stubborn stone has to give way to flowers and roots!
A shame!
choices.desc: I thought we could win this time

next.desc: The gigantic battle puppet is wholly unexhausted, and the winning snowpriest controller brandishes their wooden staff proudly. 'Next! Let's have another bout!' You can't help but feel closer to these uncharacteristically yelling and whistling Sami. The host hands you the roster again while laughing—the entire gathering no longer holds any sense of solemnity.

encounters.title: Best Buddies
encounters.desc: 'Listen to me, my dear friend...' Far away from a tribal bonfire gathering, you see a Sami man drinking alone with his arm around a tree. He sees you approaching, and asks the tree beside him with an evasive look in his eyes: 'Do you mind getting to know a stranger? No? Alright.'

choices.title: Leave
variants: Raise Collapse Value by 2, but gain 2 Hope and obtain a random Collectible

choices.title: Join them
choices.desc: Have a drink, and we'll all become friends

encounters.title: Entrance to the Abyss
encounters.desc: The research team has found a large and deep cave. It looks like a regular cavern, and looking down you can see a variety of strange plants and symbols. The team members are extremely excited, and hope to get your permission to use the Survey Equipment to probe the cave's extremities.

choices.title: Let's give it a try
choices.desc: Connect the cables well, and slowly lower the survey equipment

choices.title: Piece together the information you got and locate the new dig site

next.desc: Piecing together the clues eventually has the team digging up an artifact at the cave's edge. The container storing the object is made of an organic polymer biomaterial. There is no civilization on Terra capable of making such a material, so could this... possibly be a prehistoric remnant?
After the equipment cools down, the research team jumps back into action, eager to request permission to use the device again.

choices.title: Equipment overheated, let's take a short break
Try using your broken equipment on the spot
choices.desc: Anti-Interference Index is 0, but gain a large amount of Originium Ingots

next.desc: If the survey equipment is exhausted here, you soon won't even be able to find your way back, so you decisively reject the team's request.

encounters.title: Change
encounters.desc: Many years have passed since the giant facility at the end of the icefield was discovered, and many great things have happened since then: 'Terra Icefield Megastructure Research and Restoration Convention'—the birth of the Black Hole Protocol, the Feranmut Sami's departure, and a spike in technological advancements brought by Collapsal research. Every explorer undoubtedly dreams of traveling north to the place that changed everything, but... what exactly is the test mentioned in the invitation? That's when you notice an unfamiliar figure in camp that no one else seems to perceive.

next.desc: You subconsciously pull out the invitation and hand it over to him. He takes a look, nods, and turns around before vanishing before your eyes. You seem to catch a glimpse of the Rhodes Island logo and a bone pen as he disappears. However, the very next moment you find yourself in the sands of Sargon. Your team members are panicking at this sudden shift, but you are fully aware that this is a test for you to prove yourself.

Just as you are about to attack him, he vanishes into the mist, and the other expeditioners look to you in surprise. Luckily, the farce passes quickly, and you resume your journey towards the icefield.

encounters.title: Reconstruction
encounters.desc: By the time your squad arrives at the camp, everything else is already ready. You hand Kal'tsit the materials, and she separates them before distributing them to every department. As the instruments sound, the scientists take their positions, and the gigantic megastructure restoration project officially begins. In phase one, expeditioners have to clean up the Collapsals and eradicate their influence on the ring-shaped megastructure.

choices.title: Power it with the Boundless Gift
Bruteforce repairs, and disregard the crack in space
Solve it in the crudest way
Harbor hope with every step you take

encounters.title: One Small Step for Expeditioners
encounters.desc: One moment you were deep in the icefield, and the next you find yourself in a scorching desert. Behind you are the ruins of a megastructure identical to the one on the icefield, except almost completely eroded by the wind and sand. You consider the harsh environment ahead, and immediately send a request for help to the other end of the gate.

choices.title: Alright, let's press on
variants: Raise Collapse Value by 2, and immediately promote 2 Operators
Raise Collapse Value by 2, and immediately recruit an Operator

encounters.title: Wounded Prime Tree
encounters.desc: Holding a clan migration is uncommon to the Sami, but by no means unheard of. You are now in the midst of a migrating Sami tribe, their clan tree making long and huge strides, starting and stopping as it unceasingly waits for its clansmen and their livestock to come along. These tribespeople look exhausted but still greet you politely. Their snowpriest has already agreed to tell you about their experience so far.

choices.title: Gaze at the clan tree
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 3, but gain 2 Anti-Interference Index

encounters.title: Research Outpost
encounters.desc: You and your team members find an unmanned research outpost. Magallan skillfully opens the combination lock at the entrance and welcomes everyone inside. For now, the cold winds of Sami are far away, and you can take a breather to calm down and organize your thoughts.

choices.title: Seems like the drone's found something
choices.desc: Restore all Life Points, gain 3 Hope, and promote 1 Operator

encounters.title: A Dark Room
encounters.desc:
Following the directions left by a snowpriest on their deathbed, you find a hut lodged in the wind and snow. Your companions switch on their searchlights, but the darkness is barely dispelled. You notice traces of a snowpriest spell lingering on the light's edge. Just what did they fight here? 
The doors suddenly slam shut.

choices.title: Explore with caution
choices.desc: Fear only comes from the unknown

next.desc: An inexplicable panic grips the room, but you and your companions keep silent. You realize that some supplies have been left here, and perhaps you can find something useful for the research team.

choices.title: Leave
variants: The darkness past the door is scarier than the outside!
The thorns left by the snowpriest have already wilted

choices.title: Rummage through the depths
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 3, but obtain a random Collectible

encounters.title: Black Footprints
encounters.desc: Your teeth are chattering. Dead beasts, countless dead beasts, dirty snow covering their bodies. Intuition tells you not to go any further in this direction, but you are unable to free your legs from the quagmire of fear. You don't know how long you spend standing there until the burly local warriors have you surrounded.

choices.title: Santalla's Sanctuary
choices.desc: Restore 5 Life Points, and gain 10 Objective Shield

choices.title: Confront them without speaking
choices.desc: Talking too much in a panic will only make things worse

next.desc: They see you as a poacher, as the equipment and instruments on you are beyond suspicious in their eyes. Just what have they gone through to treat all outsiders with such violence? But now, you have no time to think about it.

choices.title: Challenge your fear
choices.desc: Encounter an even more dangerous battle

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Clairvoyance's Direction
encounters.desc:
The blizzard on the uninhabited icefield makes it impossible to see anything, and you and your research team companions fight the extreme cold as you try your best to keep up with the mysterious procession in black. Where are these Cyclopes headed to? 
They reply: 'The next millennium.'

choices.title: Leave
choices.desc: What if they predict something bad?

next.desc: You have heard many a legend about this band of Sarkaz, but you had never believed that they existed in the northern reaches of Sami until you saw them with your own eyes. You're worried that the rumors of them bringing bad luck are true, so you silently watch them walk away.

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Sami's Language
encounters.desc:
'From a linguistic perspective, deciphering the Foldartals in the hands of the locals is no puzzle to crack. The truly difficult question is where they come from, and if they were carved by man... wait, what's that sound?' 
The professionals in the research team are proposing that you set up cameras to monitor the local shamans' movements, so all of you witness this moment together: the wood's surface cracks and peels with a loud noise, and a intricate pattern forms on the tree.

encounters.title: On-site at Public Signal Amplification Station 756
encounters.desc: You find a patch of what the tribespeople call a 'glade which glows when you step on it' that seems to be an important landmark in this area. You walk to the center of the clearing, and notice that part of the surrounding snow begins to disappear into thin air, revealing streaks of spectacular light in the ground. Then, your communicator receives a signal, the first in a long time, and the voices of your other companions left behind the camp far to the south erupt from it, and it sounds like they're in complete disbelief.

choices.title: Tell them you're safe
choices.desc: The signal probably won't last much longer

next.desc: You wanted to get everyone in the team to talk to those back at camp, but then the glowing streaks in the ground quickly dim before your communications disconnect. You and your companions momentarily rest on the spot before moving on.

choices.title: Send your coordinates and call for backup
choices.desc: Gain 2 Hope, and recruit 1 Operator from any class

choices.title: Contact Kal'tsit for reinforcements
choices.desc: Gain 2 Hope and recruit an Operator, with certain Operators already promoted at the time of recruitment

encounters.title: On-site at Public Nomadic Station N6
encounters.desc: A snowdrift taller than a person appears right in front of you, and under your instruments' prompting, you and your companions unearth the corner of a half-buried building with two large doors. The lights on the doors are still lit, one green and one red, emitting a faint glow.

choices.title: Say the password
choices.desc: You hold the flyers from earlier, obtain 2 Collectibles

choices.title: Seems like Angelina has something to say
choices.desc: Gain 5 Objective Shield, 8 Hope, and 20 Originium Ingots

encounters.title: Ursus
encounters.desc: What presumably was the lair of a mangler now echoes laughter, shouting, and Ursine expletives, as some of what seem to be its new inhabitants are clearly enjoying themselves. In the corner you can see the cave's original owner, heaving its last breaths after getting punched and kicked.

choices.title: Why is this place full of Ursus?
It's clearly a King-Of-The-Hole competition!
choices.desc: Lose 1 Life Point, but gain 4 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Let them revel in their madness, we have places to be
choices.desc: You don't want to tangle with a bunch of drunks

next.desc: You and your companions leave the cave behind. You are quite sure that the local Sami tribe will not ignore this matter.

encounters.title: Useless Item
encounters.desc: You are lost, and rescued by a local shaman who seems to be used to this. She enthusiastically offers you some help, saying that she's carrying more than she needs. However, as you talk to her, your gaze cannot help but drift to the path she came from, as well as the withered vegetation on it.

choices.title: Ask for survival
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 1, but gain 3 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Ask for knowledge
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 2, but gain a random Foldartal

encounters.title: Missing Mountains
encounters.desc:
Just as you are about to make a misstep, someone pulls you backward. The mountains' shadows overlap, and a distorted darkness creeps across your eyes. In the face of your trepidation, the fortuneteller-looking woman asks you to draw a card. 
—How strange. As far as you know, neither the Sami or the Cyclopes Sarkaz use divination as a way to tell the future.

choices.title: Pick the tarot card on the left
choices.desc: Obtain Collectible 「'Peek into the Eternal Night'」

choices.title: Opt to choose nothing
choices.desc: Maybe it's better to leave everything to her

next.desc:
'May our fates cross again once more...' 
She bids you farewell, walking off into the wind and snow.

choices.title: Pick the tarot card on the right
Pick the discarded tarot card by her hand

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Forward Forest
encounters.desc: Gnarled trees and vines completely block your path, and the fates of innumerable Sami beings extend along the branches and leaves, crisscrossing and connecting before your eyes. You check over and over that the map provided by the locals truly points in this direction.

choices.title: Whip out woodcutting axe
choices.desc: Clear the trees and open a path

next.desc: You finally cut through the overgrowth of trees and vines, and something falls to the ground as the sun shines into the forest. You feel a strange premonition—you might just have damaged an exquisite artifact. But the damage done is irreversible, and you can do nothing but to pick up the fallen object and leave.

choices.title: Can't we just walk on the trees?
choices.desc: Lose 1 Anti-Interference Index, and enter the Secluded Passage (The environment will change)

encounters.title: Wilderness Survival Expert
encounters.desc: A lone explorer covered in dirt and grime asks you for today's date, and you hear him count the years in a whisper before he violently slaps himself in the face. 'Whatever, I made it back alive.' He mumbles a few Columbian expletives. 'And who tricked you into coming here? Want me to pass on a message?'

choices.title: Give a small token of appreciation
choices.desc: Lose 2 Originium Ingots, but recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Give a large reward as thanks
choices.desc: Lose 4 Originium Ingots, but recruit an Operator from any Class

choices.title: Wish him well
choices.desc: Trust that sincerity will move him

next.desc:
'You're right, luck does prove very useful in Sami.' 
He jots down your message on a piece of scrap paper, stuffs it into his backpack, and lets out a few more expletives as he leaves. You remember the name he's been cursing, and it seems to be... the Director of Rhine Lab's Scientific Investigation Section?

encounters.title: Trimounts Travel Agency Dispatch
encounters.desc: A micro-operation platform stops in front of you. Several large low-temperature storage boxes commonly used for scientific research are bound to the chassis with ropes, with a small lidded iron basket welded to a huge cryoshield. Inside are tourism flyers covered in scenic photographs of Trimounts, Bunkerhill City, and Max D.C., along with some embarrassing copywriting, claiming that their travel agency 'now has Sami-speaking tour guides!'

choices.title: Open a storage box
choices.desc: Anti-Interference Index is 0, but gain 6 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Use all your strength, the good stuff has to be down there
Try your best to shove all the useless items to the sides of the box
Take away the flyers
choices.desc: 'Come! Come see the cliffs and the desert!'

next.desc: The operation platform spins around and makes a rhythmic ticking sound, as if telling you something, but before you can even consider anything, it emits a flash of dazzling light and disappears. You and your companions look at each other in dismay, but come to a realization and you turn over the flyers in your hands—amazingly, they all have the same set of coordinates and password printed on the back.

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Auspicious Sign
encounters.desc: You discover a colony of musbeasts in a clearing. The little white creatures are bunched up together like a woolen quilt. Your presence has not scared them away, and on the contrary, you have a vague sense that they're actually inviting you.

encounters.title: Rain!
encounters.desc:
'It's black rain...
'We just finished harvesting a few samples,' the man says in a numb voice, 'but now both my samples and equipment are destroyed, and my people have run away... What do I do about the money I owe the company?'

choices.title: Hold up his detector in front of his face
Have him take a good look at his drone

encounters.title: Ceremony of Healing
encounters.desc: This funerary fleet is performing some sort of rite. Drums beat to a rhythm as people bend down to kiss the water, whispering prayers for the spirits and the sun's rays. The host invites you to do as the locals do.

choices.title: Try cautiously
choices.desc: Lose 1 Anti-Interference Index, but gain 3 Max Life Points

choices.title: Gladly join in
choices.desc: Anti-Interference Index is 0 and lose 3 Life Points, but receive a random Collectible

next.desc: You have heard of this rite that calms the mind and grants courage against evil spirits, but you still don't know what exactly these evil spirits the Sami see really are. Confident in the research team's equipment and supplies, you decline their invitation.

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Grab and Go
encounters.desc: To your knowledge, the hunters and scouts from the local tribes often travel through the forests and snowfields in groups, working for weeks out in the countryside. They dig burrows in the ground and hollow out tree trunks to store emergency supplies in. According to their customs, these supplies can be accessed as needed—the forest itself will whisper the locations of these stashes to Sami hunters. But you are different, you don't need to hear the trees' voices, for you have equipment and instruments.

encounters.title: Regular Maintenance
encounters.desc: Contrary to what southerners may imagine, there aren't actually many sunny days on the snowy plains—and this is one of those days. You and your companions seize the opportunity to conduct an inspection, setting up various instruments and equipment with everyone helping out. On a distant snowfield are mangler cubs frolicking with their prey, and a herd of hornbeasts on a migration.

choices.title: Check your locator, and tap your communicator a few times
Look through a multicolor photometric telescope
Leisurely open a can of soup
choices.desc: Lose Amma's Affection, but gain 15 Hope

encounters.title: Unfreezing River
encounters.desc: You unconsciously take a step back when you see the river. It seems to have appeared out of nowhere, blocking you as it surges down a mountain stream. You and your companions make use of the various instruments you carry, but collect no abnormal data, and have no way to explain the uneasy anxiety inside you all.

choices.title: Go around it
choices.desc: Lose 3 Hope and Max Hope, but Restore 4 Life Points

choices.title: Look for tools
choices.desc: Lose Amma's Affection, and gain a random Collectible

encounters.note: Appears only in the hidden realm, at random

encounters.title: Lowland Market
encounters.desc: A huge clan tree sits submerged in a swamp, with Sami traders all over the trunks, the roots, and by the banks. Branches move back and forth between the stalls, as animals cry out to peddle goods or transmit information. Wooden puppets trudge between the ground and swamp to store their cargo. Everything just feels so exotic!

choices.title: Barter in front of the thick branches
choices.desc: Do you know where the traders are?

choices.title: Follow the sound of roaring
choices.desc: Gain 1 Objective Shield, and encounter a battle

choices.title: Follow the sound of the horn
choices.desc: Gain 1 Objective Shield, and encounter a battle

choices.title: Help the Sami with the Foldartal
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 4, but gain an unusual Foldartal

next.desc: This place is just too strange, so you quickly urge the research team members to leave.

encounters.title: Fence of Wood and Snow
encounters.desc: In a corner of Sami lies a clearing surrounded by snow-covered thorns, and you don't know when exactly you ended up inside. The light of the twin moons descend from the sky, and although it gives off no heat, it is as dazzling as the sun. You search for shade from the bright light, but find only an old tree/a human/a giant crow. While they are far from you, their shadow reaches your feet. You need only offer a word as tribute and you will receive their shelter. You blurt out—

choices.title: 'Trade'
choices.desc: The weight of your word depends on your item

choices.title: 'Battle'
choices.desc: Gain 1 Objective Shield; the desire to fight wells up inside you

choices.title: 'Protect'
choices.desc: Gain 1 Objective Shield; you insist on protecting everything

choices.title: 'Treescar'
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 4, but gain an unusual Foldartal

next.desc: The word pops out of your mouth before you can think, and it represents your lack of understanding of Sami. And so, the thorns reach out from the snow, pushing you back to your teammates.

encounters.title: Amma Tal
encounters.desc: You had thought this was just another path made by a beast, but halfway through you realize your companions are not your team members, but a herd of hornbeasts. At the end of the path is a huge white hornbeast sleeping soundly in a flower bed. The hornbeasts frolic and graze around It, and a spring flows right by Its side. A thought suddenly pops into your mind—It does not live here, but the woodland exists because It rests here.

choices.title: Offer a Foldartal
choices.desc: I want to look for It

choices.title: Guard the woodland
choices.desc: Gain 1 Hope; Its rest mustn't be disturbed

choices.title: Defend the woodland
choices.desc: Gain 1 Hope; Its rest mustn't be disturbed

choices.title: Look for Treescar's trail
choices.desc: Raise Collapse Value by 4, but gain an unusual Foldartal

next.desc: Not wanting to disturb the hornbeast's rest, you sample a taste of the springwater flowing by Its side. It is so sweet and fresh that the misery within you seems to abate a bit. Just as you are savoring the sweetness, you are shaken awake by a teammate next to you. It seems that everything that just happened was just a dream.

encounters.title: Searching For A Path Forward
encounters.desc: An unexplored area lies before you, so you stop and prepare to select someone to scout the way ahead.

encounters.title: Vacation Spot
encounters.desc: There is still no precise answer as to why someone would invest in a resort in such a harsh and cold place like Sami. But in fact, business is booming here, and the owner is making a fortune. Additionally, as one of the research team's sponsors, he is happy to let your tired team members rest here free of charge for a night and indulge in the pleasures of Sami.

encounters.title: Crossing Sami's Arm
encounters.desc:
The research team walks up the mountain ridge, the land behind gradually fading away. The distant scenery is shrouded with wind and snow, and the only thing nearby is a single path with no end in sight. You and your teammates are walking in between, not knowing where you have come from or where you are going, merely repeating the advancement of this journey. The mountain gale roars, the falling snow rumbles, and nature questions the group—
'Do you still wish to proceed?'

next.desc: It is obvious that the environment can no longer stop mankind's footsteps, and so the mountain range raises a peak in front of everyone. Sami wishes to understand your determination.

choices.title: Put on the helmet
choices.desc: Hold the Treescar Helm with firm resolve

encounters.title: Shattered Omen
encounters.desc: The Foldartal leads you to the location of the Treescar. His clan looks to have just gone through a terrible battle, and everyone seems to have some sort of injury. But there is no one groaning or cursing. They are like the forest itself: silent, and yet powerful. You wish to go up and talk to him, and hand him the Foldartal. But after he sees the woodcut in your hands from afar, he orders the warriors to rise and continue marching across the snowfield. All that is left for you is a messy camp.

encounters.title: Pessimist's Cage
encounters.desc: The route Valarqvin drew for you leads you to a huge cave. There are no light sources at all, and Valarqvin stops you from any attempt to light a lamp or turn on a flashlight. All you can do is hold her hand and move forward step by step. When you finally see light again, you spot an altar with a flower atop: rootless, blooming, and incomparably sharp. Many Cyclopes are sitting around the altar, and they are silent as they 'stare' at the flower in front of them. 'Your path will be darker than the one you have just traveled,' Valarqvin says to herself. 'If you can still walk, then pick up the flower.' As she utters the very last word, all the Cyclopes look towards you.

exploreTools.name: Altar-type Radar
exploreTools.desc: Wireless locating device that can be quickly deployed and withdrawn to avoid friendly casualties. After returning from Chernobog, engineers have worked to understand the principles behind the device, overcoming the harm they once caused.
exploreTools.usage: Bring 1 Altar-type Radar to each battle

exploreTools.name: R-45 Portable 'Gramophone'
exploreTools.desc: Originium Arts conductor prototype, part of Engineering's attempt to create a transmission protocol that operators with lower Originium Arts Assimilation can use. Criticized by some Leithanians as lacking in elegance.
exploreTools.usage: Bring 1 R-45 Portable 'Gramophone' to each battle

exploreTools.name: Ornamental Giant Mushroom
exploreTools.desc: Manmade shelter that provides cover from the weather. Blatantly artificial, it fails to blend into the environment even in the jungles of Sargon.
exploreTools.usage: Bring 2 Ornamental Giant Mushrooms to each battle

exploreTools.name: Field Device LUD-99X
exploreTools.desc: Every operator seems to have a different idea about what this is. Some saw a stool, others a crowbar. Meanwhile, Closure and most engineering operators insist that its serial number is assigned to a conceptual model that has never been produced. Nor will it ever be.
exploreTools.usage: Field Device LUD-99X appears in all battles

exploreTools.name: Two-step Firecracker and Data-collector
exploreTools.desc: One-time data collection device that emits a loud popping sound when activated. A Yanese operator has added the following note to the manual: 'It only pops once.'
exploreTools.usage: Bring 5 Two-step Firecracker and Data-collectors to each battle

exploreTools.name: Self-Driving Recon Cart
exploreTools.desc: Autonomous device that can collect data and record video in areas too dangerous for operators to enter. Like most Durin designs, it spent a large portion of its development cycle as a toy for operators before field testing was conducted.
exploreTools.usage: Bring 1 Recon Self-Driver Launchpoint to each battle

items.name: Outcast
items.usage: Select all adjacent combat nodes to the right
items.desc: Realm-weight the guilt, ruined of pardon; never now to meet again.

items.name: Hunter
items.usage: Select 1 combat node
items.desc: Bow traps shadow, shrieking in wind; knows the hunt no end.

items.name: Warrior
items.usage: Select 1 combat node + 1 more randomly selected combat node
items.desc: Firm beeth rock, rising in ranges; horned the helmet in hoar.

items.name: Shaman
items.usage: Select 1 combat node + 2 more randomly selected combat nodes
items.desc: Speak in sage tongues, secret the cipher; call to banish the bale.

items.name: Snowpriest
items.usage: Randomly select 3 combat nodes, or 3 nodes of any type when in Secluded Passage
items.desc: Espy the realm, every life seen; seek your sojourn path.

items.name: Hero
items.usage: Select all combat nodes
items.desc: Sprawl do thorns, throttling bones; death is a dale of return.

items.name: Sing
items.usage: Allied units gain ASPD +50 in battle here
items.desc: Incants string-song, echoing round; in forest all feathers alight.

items.name: Silence
items.usage: Allied units gain Max HP +50% in battle here
items.desc: Heed the trees' way, hearken no answer; still are mountains stood.

items.name: Recite
items.usage: Recover 4 Life Points after traversing node(s)
items.desc: Speak the old kennings, pray to your kin; weave the mind of word.

items.name: Debate
items.usage: Allied units have Redeployment Time -30% in battle here
items.desc: Cease does your singing, self do I show; thought meets truth intact.

items.name: Console
items.usage: Gain 2 Hope after traversing node(s)
items.desc: Drum-beat and leaf-fire, long-lost souls hail!; whispers wean from grief.

items.name: Confess
items.usage: Recover 1 Life Point after traversing node(s); there is a hidden power yet to show itself
items.desc: Lone with the Tree, talk of remorse; fellow pleads fellow forgive.

items.name: Canopy
items.usage: Select 1 Encounter
items.desc: Lush grow the limbs, leaves all about; here do hundred-beasts dwell.

items.name: Water
items.usage: Select 1 non-combat node
items.desc: Cleansed in slush, skin and hair; placid lies the lake.

items.name: Eye
items.usage: Select 1 adjacent node to the right
items.desc: Seen and known, sought and bid; there to gloaming gone.

items.name: Arch
items.usage: Select 1 non-combat node + 2 more randomly selected non-combat nodes
items.desc: Hornbeast tracks, training of bells; vague 'til passing's place.

items.name: Light
items.usage: Randomly select 3 non-combat nodes
items.desc: Stands the torch, certain as daylight; challenges chilblain eve.

items.name: Earth
items.usage: Select all non-combat nodes
items.desc: Rise every being, return eft to roots; hewn is All from here.

items.name: Delight
items.usage: Gain 8 Originium Ingots after traversing node(s)
items.desc: Rainbow floats moonshade, rests in its jar; here now wish as you will.

items.name: Surprise
items.usage: Change node(s) to Rogue Trader
items.desc: Amid custom strange, bestowed is a gift; waver well then unite.

items.name: Rage
items.usage: 4-star or 5-star Temporary Operators will appear in recruitments here
items.desc: Ice and rock's havoc, hammer cast down; feather cannot dare crow.

items.name: Doubt
items.usage: Change node(s) to Lost and Found
items.desc: Straight path warped, where do you lead?; not a tree does know.

items.name: Anticipate
items.usage: Originium Ingots gained here are doubled
items.desc: Mind awander, where in life's dream; glimpsed in blizzard bare.

items.name: Love
items.usage: Change node(s) to Encounter; there is a hidden power yet to show itself
items.desc: Boundless the tundra, be by my body; fire be my heart aflame.

items.name: Originium
items.usage: Select 1 node
items.desc: All mud and flesh, failing welcome; all image in all midst.

items.name: Tree
items.usage: Select 1 node with vertical paths
items.desc: Sail there high, star apparent; see the frost unfold below.

items.name: Stone
items.usage: Randomly select 2 nodes with vertical paths
items.desc: Barren of life, bare of a shift; silent sits antiquity.

items.name: Shrub
items.usage: Select all nodes in the adjacent column to the right
items.desc: Prone do you slink, prowler and beast; there you hear the horn.

items.name: Beast
items.usage: Select 1 node, + 2 more randomly selected nodes
items.desc: Gnaw upon soil, steal fruits of rime; move as may you wish.

items.name: Human
items.usage: Select 1 node and all connected nodes around it
items.desc: Animate wisdom, willed to the young; down the ages tie the days.

items.name: Patrol
items.usage: Conduct Panoptic Survey on nearby node(s), Collapse value reduction will be 2 when combined
items.desc: Trees scaled at day, twilight in lookout; kept is earth by fire of kin.

items.name: Nest
items.usage: Gain 2 Objective Shield after traversing node(s)
items.desc: Gather wild timber, tell of shade-shelter; no chill 'neath eaves blows.

items.name: Hunt
items.usage: Change node(s) to Emergency Ops
items.desc: Tusk and claw, time bode well; learn to dare on danger's path.

items.name: Plunder
items.usage: Gain 1 Originium Ingot, Hope, and Objective Shield after traversing node(s)
items.desc: Wrest and plunder, righteous not; preordained are dues to pay.

items.name: Reproduce
items.usage: Change node(s) to Scout
items.desc: Flora and fauna, flourish and fall; last ye everlong.

items.name: Migrate
items.usage: Gain 1 Anti-Interference Index after traversing node(s); there is a hidden power yet to show itself
items.desc: Out of snow's forest, far from tribe's tree; yearn you harder yet.

items.name: Advent
items.usage: Select 1 node, and a Concord will trigger
items.desc: Cross mount and stream, snows thus traversed; fate's choice fixed in stone.

items.name: Departure
items.usage: Randomly select 2 nodes, and a Concord will trigger
items.desc: Far lies the sound, sight flung in distance; unknowns yet unknown.

items.name: Cry
items.usage: Immediately recover 2 Life Points for every node selected
items.desc: Cry mournful lay, cold heavens shake; from lone trunk does forest bloom.

items.name: Satisfy
items.usage: Gain 4 Originium Ingots for every node selected
items.desc: Stores flush with hunger-guard, slaked is your thirst; naught more need ask.

items.name: Respite
items.usage: Change node(s) into Safe House
items.desc: Blind and low, lost in dreams; respite from this realm.

items.name: Scar
items.usage: Select 1 adjacent node to the right, can only be paired with 'Void'
items.desc: Cataclysm courses land, souls of travelers stirred thick

items.name: Void
items.usage: Change node to Shattered Omen, can only be paired with 'Scar'
items.desc: Realmbound deeds disappear, minds return unto the void

items.name: De-quantification
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Choosing to spend Hope or Originium Ingots and increasing your Collapse value in non-combat nodes will have a 25% chance to increase the cost by 1
〔Stage 2 · Intensified De-quantification〕 Choosing to spend Hope or Originium Ingots and increasing your Collapse value in non-combat nodes will have a 50% chance to increase the cost by 2
items.desc:
The boundaries between numbers in this area have become blurred. Humans have lost the ability to recognize natural numbers.
〔Intensified De-quantification〕 The concept of numbers itself has become indistinct, and humans have completely lost the ability to use numbers at all.

items.name: Substantial Collapse
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 4 instances of [Dominion] will appear in battle, allied units on affected tiles have -50 ASPD
〔Stage 2 · Propagating Collapse〕 8 instances of [Dominion] will appear in battle, allied units on affected tiles have -75 ASPD
items.desc:
Isolated phenomena known as 'Dominion' have begun to appear frequently on the land.
〔Propagating Collapse〕 The 'Dominion' phenomena is currently spreading rapidly, with an efficiency already near atmospheric circulation itself.

items.name: Non-linear Movement
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 All [Collapsals] gain Max HP +15% and Movement Speed +15%
〔Stage 2 · Non-linear Action〕 All [Collapsals] gain Max HP +20%, Movement Speed +20%, and ASPD +20
items.desc:
The shortest distance between two points is not a straight line.
〔Non-linear Action〕 The fastest path of descent is not a line between two points.

items.name: Emotional Entity
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Enemies have a 5% chance to appear on the battlefield with +100% Max HP
〔Stage 2 · Terrifying Entity〕 Enemies have a 10% chance to appear on the battlefield with +100% Max HP
items.desc:
When inner emotional fluctuations become nourishment and a stimulant to the enemy...
〔Terrifying Entity〕 When innermost negative emotions become an actual monster...

items.name: Pan-Society Paradox
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Rogue Trader's prices are increased by 25%
〔Stage 2 · Pan-Civilization Paradox〕 Rogue Trader's prices are increased by 50%, with the price of relatively Rare items increased by 100%
items.desc:
The definition of society has been rewritten, and the value of social products has changed as a result.
〔Pan-Civilization Paradox〕 The essence of civilization has been twisted, and the value of all products has become incapable of being explained with common sense.

items.name: Barometric Anomaly
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Natural DP regeneration rate is reduced by 20%
〔Stage 2 · Barometric Disorder〕 Natural DP regeneration rate is reduced by 40%
items.desc:
Atmospheric pressure is shifting within a range at random, and the movements of non-military crew become sluggish, unable to adapt to such fluctuations.
〔Barometric Disorder〕 Atmospheric pressure no longer adheres to any rule, and all matter has unavoidably become redundant from individual distress.

items.name: Injury Trigger
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 All allied units lose 30% HP on deployment (Effect only triggers once before unit leaves the field)
〔Stage 2 · Crisis Trigger〕 All allied units lose 60% HP on deployment (Effect only triggers once before unit leaves the field)
items.desc:
Life is the premise, and also the price for action.
〔Crisis Trigger〕 Life is the premise for action, and will also soon become the premise for death.

items.name: Convergence Consumption
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 When Anti-Interference Index decreases, Max Life Points decreases by 1
〔Stage 2 · Convergence Deficiency〕 When Anti-Interference Index decreases, Max Life Points decreases by 2
items.desc:
Whenever an instrument undergoes wear and tear, its user will feel the same kind of pain.
〔Convergence Deficiency〕 An instrument is like a part of the user's body, the wear and tear on the machine equivalent to the damage its user suffers.

items.name: Partial Cecity
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Life Points and the HP of enemies on the battlefield are harder to discern
〔Stage 2 · Complete Cecity〕 Life Points and the HP of enemies on the battlefield are harder to discern, and all enemies gain Max HP +10%
items.desc:
Your brain begins to 'naturally' skip some information, and this makes you feel comfortable.
〔Complete Cecity〕 If you can't see it, it doesn't exist. If it doesn't exist, you don't have to think about it. It feels truly great to be so carefree with nary a worry.

items.name: Image Corruption
items.usage:
〔Stage 1〕 Allied units are harder to discern
〔Stage 2 · Image Blackout〕 Allied units are harder to discern, and Deployment Cost +1
items.desc:
The PRTS operation log is not reflecting any system errors, but for some reason the Operators have been removed from the image.
〔Image Blackout〕 Even though the present situation has put a burden on your logistics work, PRTS insists that it is working normally, which is not normal.
